They are looking to hire a senior risk professional with around 10 – 12 years’ experience gained working within an alternative investment environment. The right candidate would have the ability to l
data.jd_text:
Madison Pearl is working closely with a leading global alternative investor headquartered in the GCC. They are looking to hire a senior risk professional with around 10 – 12 years’ experience gained working within an alternative investment environment. The right candidate would have the ability to lead risk management efforts across investment, operational and capital.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Lead risk management for the institution across financial risk, operational risk and capital risk.
* Lead risk oversight across financial services, products, funds, capital and liquidity management, business continuity planning, regulatory risk and compliance.
* Risk Reporting for ICAAP and preparing risk dashboards for governance bodies, including the board risk committee and fiduciary committee.
* Oversee the consolidation and recommend the departmental budget and monitor financial performance versus the budget.
* Supervise third-party/vendor risk management and cyber resilience assessments.

**Key Requirements:**

* 10 – 12 years’ experience within a buy side institution across financial and non-financial risk.
* In depth experience to private markets investment management.
* A deep familiarity to a regulatory environment such as DIFC or ADGM.
* Bachelor’s or master’s degree in financial, Economics, Risk or related field.
* CFA or FRM are highly advantageous.

**If you are interested in discussing this mandate, please contact Damion Cubitt on damion.cubitt@madisonpearl.com**

**\*Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted\***

data.title: Procurement Lead - UAEN Only
data.raw_snippet:
Manage and lead the vertical of non-technology in CPD globally and execute strategic sourcing initiatives for non tech categories HR services, FM ,Marketing, professional services. Lead with responsibility and accountability for:

1. Lead, Mentor and Develop a team of professionals on strategic sour
data.jd_text:
Manage and lead the vertical of non-technology in CPD globally and execute strategic sourcing initiatives for non tech categories HR services, FM ,Marketing, professional services. Lead with responsibility and accountability for:

1. Lead, Mentor and Develop a team of professionals on strategic sourcing
2. Assign the new projects / RFPs within the nontechnology procurement team for HR services, Facilities management marketing, professional services.
3. Build and maintain strong relationships with suppliers to ensure quality, cost effectiveness, and reliable service delivery.
4. Understand business requirements; ensure CPD function performs to its expected outcome in terms of quality, quantity, vendor / supplier satisfaction, end user satisfaction and bottom-line contribution.
5. Resolve stakeholder queries and concern.
6. Explore new and innovative ideas for automation, modernizing and simplifying the process along with cost effective and value-added approach.
7. Manage the procurement workflow and ensuring the team is issuing purchase orders accordingly
8. Optimize the procure to pay process to enhance efficiency

**Key Result Areas :**

* Ensure adherence to organization procurement policies & ethical standards
* Negotiation skills on non tech projects
* Develop and implement category strategies of assigned non-tech spend areas
* Prepare and present regular reports on procurement metrics, spend analysis and performance.
* Lead and mentor a diverse global procurement team, fostering a collaborative and high-performance culture.
* Provide training and development opportunities to enhance team skills and career growth.
* Set performance goals and KPIs for team members, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives.
* Assign responsibilities, manage workload and ensure the team achieves key performance indicators (KPI)
* Lead initiatives to reduce costs, improve accuracy and enhance operational efficiency through releasing purchase orders in a timely manner.
* Develop and Implement Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) for transactional procurement activities.
* Provide wide visibility across the bank of Procurement Policy and Centralized Procurement Department’s role so that the third-party risk is effectively managed;
* Identify areas for automation and process improvement in control and management of third party spend;
* Lending Support to Internal Stakeholders in case of escalation related to Contract disputes whether leading to legal, penal or termination actions;
* In case of the grievance amounts to allegation of fraud and/or corruption, in consultation with VP – Global Procurement & Operations Lead , shall refer the matter to the Head of Fraud Prevention & Intelligence for investigation and resolution in accordance with the policies and procedures established by that Office;
* Compile, verify accuracy and submit reports in accordance with agreed deliverables with VP – Global Procurement & Operations Lead.



**Operating Environment, Framework and Boundaries, Working Relationships**

* Reporting to VP – Global Procurement & Operations Lead – Centralized Procurement, Corporate Affairs.
* Team work, punctuality, innovation, willingness to take more responsibility.
* Increase of process efficiency, value added service.
* Oversee the supplier selection and evaluation processes as well as the development of suppliers
* Continuous adherence and improvement of process in operational procurement.
* Adherence to Procurement Policy, Bank’s operating process and procedures must maintain confidentiality of banks and vendor’s information, documents, etc.
* Escalation of internal misconduct, breach of code of ethics, confidentiality, etc.

**Problem Solving**

* Handling of customer complaints concerning the non compliance of with SLAs, TAT.
* Meet the end users and explain the importance of each line of iProc and train the users
* Coordination of user support for eProcurement Systems.
* Identify strategic risks in specific sourcing (internal/ external supply market risks);
* Ability to ensure compliance to Procurement Policies and to find solutions.

**Decision Making Authority & Responsibility**

* Authority to set team goals, delegate tasks, and make staffing decisions within the procurement team, including hiring, performance evaluations, and professional development opportunities.
* Responsibility for setting Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and determining team objectives aligned with overall company procurement goals.
* Authority to recommend or implement procurement process improvements, including digitalization and automation tools, to enhance efficiency and reduce operational costs.
* Responsible for managing the end-to-end transactional procurement function, ensuring efficient purchasing processes and compliance with company policies and international regulations.
* Accountable for driving cost reduction initiatives and achieving procurement savings.
* Accountable for team training, mentoring, and fostering a collaborative, results-driven culture within the procurement department.

**Knowledge, Skills and Experience**

* Business Graduate with preferably Procurement Certifications like CIPS, SPSM etc.;
* Minimum 8-10 years of experience leading procurement function with last 4 years managing a similar position in a service / banking industry.
* Familiar with local market dynamics in particular and regional / international in general.
* Strong interpersonal skills and cultural sensitivity.
* Strong contract development and negotiations skills including exposure to ERP, B2B Systems, tenders, eAuctions etc.
* Relationship management, people management and networking skills
* Communication, negotiation & management skills.

data.jd_text:
**Creatio is a global vendor of an AI-native platform**
to automate workflows and CRM with no-code and a maximum degree of freedom. Our platform combines an AI-first architecture, composable no-code tools, and enterprise-grade governance to help organizations build and scale faster.
  
  
We’re proud to be recognized by top industry analysts as a
**Leader and Strong Performer in multiple Gartner and Forrester reports.**
**In 2025, Creatio was named to Inc.’s Best Workplaces list,**
recognizing our commitment to employee wellbeing and a strong workplace culture.
  
  
**The Opportunity**
For the role of
**Enterprise Account Executive (Middle East)**
, we are seeking an experienced field sales professional to help us scale the number of Enterprises that use the Creatio platform. Candidates must have experience building and developing a territory to high performance within an innovative SaaS technology provider. Successful candidates will help to build a portfolio of business within the top target enterprises in their territory. Successful candidates will need to drive their own activity and build a funnel while being supported by the broader Creatio organization of diverse and executive, product, and technical teams.
  
  
The role is based in the
**Middle East (Dubai)**
.
  
  
Our targets are ambitious, and we are seeking engaging, collaborative, successful Enterprise Account Executives who have the skills, experience, and track record to make an immediate impact. We’re fast-paced, innovative, and collaborative. We pursue excellence in everything and have a lot of fun along the way.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Deliver against revenue targets and all key performance metrics
* Generate new business opportunities on an assigned territory, manage pipeline, opportunities, forecasting, and other items related to a normal outside sales function
* Manage the end-to-end enterprise sales process and resource to create and execute winning solution sales strategies
* Establish relationships with VP/C-level buyers (Business and IT)
* Develop and orchestrate sales presentations and product demonstrations
* Focus on new logo acquisition and manage relationships with the existing accounts to identify opportunities for cross and up-sales
* Ability to work with all customer functions including executive-level personnel, various customer stakeholders, architecture, operations, design, vendor management, innovation, sourcing, and customer procurement

**Desired Skills And Experience**

* Minimum 5 years of experience working within enterprise SaaS platform sales to large enterprises
* Proven track record of meeting quarterly and annual targets
* Deep understanding of executive-level Enterprise and/or Global Account sales and methodologies
* Superb written and verbal communications and organizational skills
* 4-year degree required, with technical curiosity and desire to learn
* Experience with CRM/BPM software space is strongly preferred
* Fluency in English and Arabic

**What You Should Expect From Us**

* Powerful award-winning product
* Focus on making our clients successful (NPS score – 42%)
* Dedicated leadership with open doors
* A steady stream of inbound leads (though we expect you to generate your own leads as well)
* A collaborative environment where everybody is happy to help

data.jd_text:
**VP Customer Success & Operations- B2B Fintech & Trading**

Our client is hiring a
**VP Customer Success & Operations**
to build a repeatable global operating system that supports scale, delivery excellence, and commercial execution across a fast-growing fintech infrastructure business. This is a pivotal leadership role, working closely with the CEO and cross-functional teams to productize the company’s offering, improve delivery capability, and reduce dependency on the CTO across presales, delivery, and launch operations.

What you’ll be responsible for:

**Productization & commercial packaging**

* Convert a broad capability set into 2–4 flagship product lines with clear inclusions, exclusions, pricing logic, and implementation timelines
* Define standard deployment models across SaaS, private cloud, and hybrid, including the operational, security, and support implications of each
* Build product factsheets and sales enablement materials covering features, security posture, integrations, launch timelines, and support models

**GTM operations**

* Own sales operating cadence, including CRM stages, forecasting, win/loss feedback loops, and pipeline hygiene
* Build a deal desk with clear pricing governance, discount rules, SOW templates, change-order models, and margin controls
* Standardise presales solutioning so technical leadership is not required in every commercial conversation, using discovery templates, architecture diagrams, integration checklists, and compliance/security packs

**Delivery operations**

* Build and lead a delivery function covering Implementation Project Managers, Solution Engineers, QA/UAT, and Release Management
* Establish and embed a gated delivery methodology across discovery, solution design, integration, UAT, launch, and hypercare
* Enforce scope control through acceptance criteria, delivery exit gates, and structured change-order processes

**Compliance-by-design & risk controls**

* Own partner selection and integration readiness across key operational components
* Support liquidity aggregation and trading technology capabilities across crypto, FX/CFDs, and broader multi-asset environments
* Oversee wallet/custody components, treasury controls, and tokenisation workflows where relevant
* Ensure operational readiness aligns with security expectations, access controls, and regulated client requirements

**Customer success operating model**

* Build a structured post-launch operating model covering onboarding, hypercare, monthly operational reviews, QBRs, and renewal/expansion motions
* Implement and track key metrics such as time-to-launch, incident rate, SLA compliance, adoption, and net revenue retention
* Work cross-functionally to ensure a seamless customer experience from presales through to launch and long-term growth

**Leadership & operational scale**

* Build a scalable operating model across GTM, delivery, and platform operations
* Lead cross-functional operating cadence across Sales, Product, Engineering, and Support
* Drive consistency, accountability, and operational discipline across multi-region teams and enterprise-grade delivery environments

Skills:

* 5–7+ years’ experience in
  **fintech, trading infrastructure, brokerage, or exchange technology**
* Proven experience building or running
  **GTM, Delivery, and Platform Operations**
  within a
  **B2B product company**
* Strong technical literacy across trading platform architecture, order and risk concepts, APIs, and market data feeds
* Deep understanding of multi-asset operational environments, including digital and traditional asset classes
* Strong grasp of security, access control, and the expectations of regulated clients
* Experience scaling global operations across multi-region teams and integration-heavy enterprise environments
* Commercially minded with the ability to balance strategic thinking and hands-on execution
* Highly operational, structured, and able to build repeatable systems in fast-growth environments
* Confident working cross-functionally with senior stakeholders across commercial and technical teams
* Comfortable bringing clarity, process, and accountability to complex delivery and launch environments
* Resilient, pragmatic, and able to operate effectively in a high-growth business undergoing scale and transformation

data.title: Office Administrator - French Speaker
data.raw_snippet: As the Office Administrator at the Regional Office, you will provide administrative and coordination support to the Vice President – Operations, while assisting the Office Manager in overseeing the day-to-day administration of the Corporate Office and its visitors. This role is responsible for ensur
data.jd_text:
As the Office Administrator at the Regional Office, you will provide administrative and coordination support to the Vice President – Operations, while assisting the Office Manager in overseeing the day-to-day administration of the Corporate Office and its visitors. This role is responsible for ensuring smooth office operations through effective communication, travel coordination, report preparation, tracker management, and general administrative support.
  
  
You will play a key role in facilitating coordination between the Vice President - Operations and hotel teams, ensuring timely follow-up on operational matters, preparation of documents, tracking of deadlines, and management of logistics required for daily business activities. The position also supports the consolidation of operational reports from hotels, communication with hotel owners, coordination related to audits and hotel openings, and the organization of key operational meetings.
  
  
**Key Role Responsibilities**

* Provide administrative support to the VP – Operations, including calendar management, meeting coordination, correspondence, and document preparation.
* Support operational communication and follow-up between the Corporate Office, hotel General Managers, owners, and other stakeholders to ensure timely execution of actions.
* Prepare, format, and organize reports, presentations, meeting materials, minutes, owner documents, business review packs, and operational summaries.
* Maintain accurate and well-organized records of operational communications, approvals, action logs, and key documentation.
* Consolidate updates relating to hotel reviews, owner matters, audits, openings, and operational meetings for leadership review.
* Maintain and update operational trackers, action logs, and follow-up records to ensure information is current, accurate, and properly filed.
* Monitor deadlines and proactively follow up with hotels and internal departments to obtain required updates, documents, and inputs on time.
* Track outstanding action items across properties and functions, ensuring timely closure.
* Escalate overdue items, delays, or missing submissions to the VP – Operations where necessary.
* In coordination with the Office Manager, arrange travel for corporate executives, including flights, accommodation, transportation, and itineraries.
* Coordinate travel schedules for hotel visits, operational meetings, and regional reviews.
* Track travel plans, visit outcomes, related expenses, and post-visit follow-up actions.
* Support the flow of information between the VP – Operations, hotels, and regional teams on operational updates, requests, and follow-up actions.
* Assist in drafting and issuing internal operational communications where required.
* Liaise with relevant stakeholders to ensure requested information and updates are submitted within agreed timelines.
* Assist in collecting operational data and preparing reports for the VP – Operations.
* Support the consolidation of updates and documents arising from meetings, audits, hotel reviews, and property visits.
* Maintain organized records of reports, correspondence, and operational documentation.
* Provide general administrative and coordination support to ensure smooth day-to-day operational administration.
* Support operations teams with basic administrative tasks related to systems access requests, documentation, and coordination.
* Assist with maintaining records related to operational processes and reporting requirements.
* Provide general coordination support to help ensure continuity and efficiency across day-to-day operational administration.
* Work closely with the Office Manager on office organization, meeting coordination, visitor arrangements, and general administrative support across the office.
* Manage the reception area of the Corporate Office, ensuring it is professional, presentable, and welcoming at all times.
* Serve as the first point of contact for visitors, guests, and external partners, greeting and assisting them in a courteous and professional manner.
* Handle incoming calls, direct inquiries appropriately, and ensure messages are relayed in a timely manner.
* Coordinate visitor arrivals and support meeting room readiness for internal and external meetings.
* Support the Office Manager in the smooth day-to-day administration of the Corporate Office.
* Assist the Office Manager with day-to-day office coordination to help ensure continuity, efficiency, and a high standard of presentation across the regional office.
* Monitor the office supply requirements, including stationery, pantry items, and general office consumables, ensuring timely replenishment and maintaining appropriate stock levels for the smooth day-to-day running of the regional office.

**Candidate Requirements**

* 2–4 years of experience in office administration, executive assistance, operational coordination, or administrative support
* Previous experience supporting a senior leader in a corporate, regional, or hotel environment
* Hospitality or multi-property experience is an advantage
* Bilingual in French and English, with strong verbal and written communication skills in both languages
* Strong coordination skills across calendars, meetings, travel, reports, and follow-up activities
* Highly organized with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities at once
* Experience maintaining trackers, action logs, records, and documentation accurately
* Confident preparing presentations, meeting packs, minutes, and business correspondence
* Professional communication skills with the ability to liaise effectively with internal and external stakeholders
* Good command of Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and Outlook
* Professional appearance and manner, with confidence in handling visitors and reception responsibilities
* Discreet and trustworthy when handling confidential information
* Able to work at pace, follow up firmly, and support the smooth day-to-day running of a regional office

As VP of Engineering, you will act as the organisation’s top engineering authority, with full ownership of the function, spanning exec
data.jd_text:
Our client is looking for a
**VP of Software Engineering.**
This position is designed for a senior engineering leader who thrives in
**transformation environments.**
As VP of Engineering, you will act as the organisation’s top engineering authority, with full ownership of the function, spanning execution, organisational design, global delivery models, AI integration, platform strategy, and capability building.

**1. Engineering Operations & Delivery**

* Optimise the software development lifecycle (SDLC) end-to-end
* Improve QA processes, release cycles, and production reliability
* Increase deployment efficiency and code quality standards
* Introduce clear metrics, accountability, and delivery discipline

**2. Squad Design & Team Restructuring**

* Restructure the existing team into 3–5 high-performing squads
* Define roles, responsibilities, and squad ownership models
* Identify skill gaps, underperformance, and restructuring needs
* Balance upskilling vs selective replacement within budget constraints

**3. Engineering Performance Uplift**

* Drive a culture of high standards and accountability
* Improve output per engineer without increasing headcount
* Mentor Engineering Managers and senior engineers
* Build a clear understanding of who owns what and why

**4. Technical Improvement Priorities**

* Stabilise and improve payment systems
* Drive architecture improvements and modernisation
* Build/upgrade data infrastructure for analytics and decision-making
* Implement iterative, pragmatic rebuild strategies (not big bang)

**We are seeking an engineering leader who has the following:**

* 15+ years in software engineering, with at least five years in a senior engineering leadership role (VP, Head of Engineering, or equivalent) spanning multiple teams or geographies.
* Proven track record of transforming underperforming teams
* Strong experience managing multiple squads (3–5+)
* Deep understanding of engineering operations & SDLC optimisation
* Hands-on technical background (able to challenge architecture decisions)
* Hands-on involvement in AI/ML engineering programmes or AI-driven developer productivity initiatives.
* Experience in consumer technology, marketplace, or multi-sided platform businesses strongly preferred.

data.title: General Manager - Commercial (MEA)
data.raw_snippet:
**The AI-powered OS**
**for beauty,**
**wellness**
**and self-care**
**About Fresha**
Fresha is the AI-powered operating system for the global beauty, wellness and self-care industry, connecting and powering everything from salons and barbers to spas, medspas, fitness studios and health practices.
data.jd_text:
**The AI-powered OS**
**for beauty,**
**wellness**
**and self-care**
**About Fresha**
Fresha is the AI-powered operating system for the global beauty, wellness and self-care industry, connecting and powering everything from salons and barbers to spas, medspas, fitness studios and health practices.
  
  
Trusted by millions of consumers and businesses worldwide. Fresha is used by 140,000+ businesses and 450,000+ stylists and professionals worldwide, processing over 1 billion appointments to date.
  
  
The company is headquartered in London, United Kingdom, with 15 global offices located across North America, EMEA and APAC.
  
  
Fresha allows consumers to discover, book and pay for beauty and wellness appointments with local businesses via its marketplace, while beauty and wellness businesses and professionals use an all-in-one platform to manage their entire operations with an intuitive business software and financial technology solutions.
  
  
Fresha’s ecosystem gives merchants everything they need to run their business seamlessly by facilitating appointment bookings, point-of-sale, customer records management, marketing automation, loyalty, beauty products inventory and team management.
  
  
The consumer marketplace unlocks revenue potential for partner businesses by leveraging the power of online bookings and automated marketing through mobile apps and advanced integrations with major tech brands including Instagram, Facebook and Google.
  
  
**Role Overview**
We’re looking for an outstanding General Manager to take full ownership of our MEA business which is one of the most exciting and commercially important regions at Fresha. This is a senior leadership role where you’ll be trusted to shape strategy, drive performance and lead a growing team to new levels of success.
  
  
You’ll report directly to the CEO and work closely with senior leaders across the company. From setting the commercial vision to executing day-to-day operations, you’ll be at the centre of it all - accountable for delivering results and building a high-performing, highly motivated team.
  
  
This is a role for someone who thrives on both strategy and execution. You’ll bring structure, pace and clarity, create a culture built on ownership and high standards, and lead with energy, purpose and visibility. If you’re ready to take on a true leadership challenge in a fast-scaling tech business, this is your opportunity.
  
  
**Sales Leadership and Culture Building**

* Lead a high-performing MEA Business Development and Account Management team
* Build an elite sales culture grounded in ownership, accountability and performance
* Establish clear team rituals that drive motivation and consistency

**Team Structuring and Scaling**

* Design and scale the MEA commercial team structure, including hiring and managing through managers
* Implement performance frameworks, operating rhythms, and clear accountability measures
* Create conditions for others to lead and grow, rather than relying solely on ICs

**Commercial Strategy and Execution**

* Define and execute the go-to-market strategy for the MEA, aligned with company priorities
* Act on market feedback and business performance to adjust plans and identify opportunities
* Own the sales funnel from prospecting through activation and retention, using tools like HubSpot to manage forecasting and pipeline

**Stakeholder Management and Influence**

* Act as the voice of the MEA region within the business, ensuring local context is well understood
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to shape campaigns, products and initiatives that drive partner success
* Communicate with clarity, challenge constructively and lead through authenticity

**Hands-On Leadership**

* Lead from the front: join calls, support negotiations, and coach team members through challenges
* Set a tone of urgency, high standards, and practical support — visible, approachable and grounded in action
* Celebrate wins, course-correct when needed, and hold the team to high expectations

**What We're Looking For**

* Senior leadership experience within a SaaS, marketplace or technology business
* Proven track record of managing and scaling commercial teams, including managing through managers
* Experience leading sales and account management functions in SMB-focused environments
* Skilled at using performance data to guide decisions, track progress and motivate teams
* Structured and strategic, with the ability to balance long-term planning and near-term delivery
* Clear and direct communicator who leads with purpose and clarity
* Comfortable being hands-on — from coaching teams to joining partner calls when needed
* Embraces a “work hard, play hard” environment with high expectations and strong team spirit

**Interview Process**

* Screen Call - Video-call with Talent Team (30 - 45 mins)
* 1st Stage - Video-call with VP of Revenue Operations (45 mins)
* 2nd Stage - Video-call presentation with Leadership team (90 mins)
* Final Stage - Video Call with C-Suite

We aim to complete the entire interview process and deliver feedback within 4 weeks
  
  
Every job application received is reviewed manually by our talent team. While we strive to assess applications within 7 days, the sheer volume of talented individuals expressing interest may occasionally extend this timeframe
  
  
**Inclusive workforce**
At Fresha, we are creating a culture where individuals of all backgrounds feel comfortable.
  
  
We want all Fresha people to feel included and truly empowered to contribute fully to our vision and goals. Everyone who applies will receive fair consideration for employment.
  
  
We do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, age, marital status, gender identity, national origin, disability, or any other applicable legally protected characteristics in the location in which the candidate is applying.
  
  
If you have any accessibility requirements that would make you more comfortable during the interview process and/or once you join, please let us know so that we can support you.
  
  
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.title: Director of Human Resources (AMEA)

**Your role at Clorox:**

data.jd_text:
**Your role at Clorox:**

The Director of HR will lead the strategic people agenda across the AMEA region (Africa, Middle East, Europe, Australia/New Zealand, Greater China, Korea, and South East Asia), a diverse and dynamic geography that includes multiple joint venture partnerships in Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Korea, and Kenya. Reporting directly to the VP & General Manager, AMEA, and partnering closely with the Senior HR Director, H&H Group, this role serves as the senior HR leader for the region and a key member of the AMEA Leadership Team. In this highly visible position, the Director of HR will act as a trusted advisor to the VP GM and regional leaders, bringing strong business acumen, credibility, and perspective to help shape decisions that impact both people and performance. The role has end‑to‑end accountability for the people agenda across commercial and manufacturing operations, including direct leadership of the HR agenda for the Saudi Arabia joint venture and strategic guidance to JV partners across the region.

This leader will ensure that current and future talent, capability, and organizational needs are met by effectively embedding Clorox talent programs, rewards, and people processes where available, and thoughtfully designing local solutions where they are not—always aligned to the ethos and spirit of Clorox while meeting regional requirements. The scope includes full responsibility for core HR programs such as talent acquisition, learning and development, performance management, organizational design, rewards and benefits, employee relations, compliance, health and wellness, payroll, and employee communications. Working closely with AMEA leaders and International HR counterparts, the Director of HR will design and execute a people strategy that enables the AMEA business strategy, supports joint venture success, and delivers sustainable results in a complex, multicultural, and fast‑moving environment.

**In this role, you will:**

**Strategy Development and Execution**

* Member of the HRBP community of practice, developing and executing against the enterprise people strategy.
* Member of AMEA Leadership Team, developing and executing the AMEA SBU strategy.
* Take primary ownership for the development and roll out of AMEA’s and Joint Venture’s People Strategy across a large geographical remit, including down to country and site level.
* Lead strategic people initiatives in support of broader AMEA business strategy.
* Lead the workforce planning process within the region. Lead integration of Clorox people strategies and implement within the region where applicable and adds value. Where Clorox strategies are not available, personally lead the identification and delivery in line with business needs (especially within the JV businesses).
* Identify, agree, define and implement culture and engagement programs at regional level
* Maintain communications and linkage to Clorox HR
* Act as lead in People agenda for M&A related activities, including identification, due diligence and post M&A integration.

**Operations, Efficiency and Compliance**

* Ensure HR processes are competitive programs and structures are in place and integrated across business.
* Ensure controls and standards are in place for all people related aspects of our business, including compliance payroll, employment standards, local regulations.
* Escalation point for employment matters, which are all non-routine/complex given the nature of the AMEA business
* Influence, prepare and deliver the HR budget across for AMEA SBU

**HR consultant**

* HR leader for AMEA leadership team, providing HR perspective on business discussions.
* Go beyond ‘meeting service requests but act as a catalyst for change, and coach LT members using broad business experience.
* Understand business challenges/opportunities (both internally and externally) and support AMEA leaders in driving culture, as well as acting as an equal face of the the business leadership to local teams (beyond HR).
* Provide guidance, coaching, and thought leadership to AMEA leaders and managers
* Provide Clorox HR expertise and guidance on JV projects and ensure projects/priorities are coordinated and aligned to the broader strategy. Where Management Control sits with Clorox, personally direct the delivery of work, and be wholly accountable for it, when it comes to HR.
* Provide feedback, and actively engage stakeholders within Clorox HR, to ensure the uniqueness, is considered and incorporated in relevant Clorox/JV HR initiatives.

**People Development**

Lead, manage, and develop HR staff in the region.

**What we look for:**

* 10+ years of progressive HR management experience, including at least 5 years in senior HR leadership roles within complex, multi‑national organizations; FMCG experience strongly preferred.
* Proven senior HR generalist with end‑to‑end experience across the full HR lifecycle, from payroll and employee relations through strategy, organizational design, and transformation.
* Demonstrated success acting as a trusted advisor and strategic partner to executive and senior leadership, influencing decisions beyond the HR function.
* Strong hands‑on expertise across all core HR disciplines, including Compensation & Benefits, Talent Acquisition, Learning & Development, Communications, Employee Relations, and Workforce Planning.
* Deep working knowledge of international labor laws, employment practices, and local ordinances across multiple jurisdictions (e.g., Middle East, Saudi Arabia, Asia), including experience managing unionized workforces.
* Extensive experience within Joint Venture and complex corporate entity structures, including board governance, statutory duties, and compliance obligations. (HIGHLY PREFERRED)
* Experience supporting high‑growth, scale‑up environments, including entering new markets and building HR operating models in emerging or evolving markets.
* Background supporting manufacturing businesses and frontline/manufacturing workforces; working knowledge or qualification in Health & Safety Management is highly preferred.
* Demonstrated expertise in managing local litigation and employee relations matters in close partnership with Legal.
* Ability to operate independently and “self‑serve” in environments with limited corporate infrastructure or centralized support.
* Strong business, commercial, and strategic acumen, with the ability to translate business strategy into executable people initiatives.
* Comfortable navigating ambiguity, competing priorities, and ad‑hoc demands while consistently driving results and process improvement.
* Highly effective communicator and influencer with strong consultation, critical thinking, and stakeholder management skills; able to adapt approach to diverse cultural contexts.
* Proven people leader with strong coaching, development, and team management capabilities.
* University degree required; advanced degree in Human Resources, Business, or Behavioral Sciences preferred. Fluency in English (written and verbal) required; Arabic is a plus but not required.

#LI-HYBRID

**Workplace type:**

3 days in office, 2 days work from home office

**Our values-based culture connects to our purpose and empowers people to be their best, professionally and personally. We serve a diverse consumer base which is why we believe teams that reflect our consumers bring fresh perspectives, drive innovation, and help us stay attuned to the world around us. That’s why we foster an inclusive culture where every person can feel respected, valued, and fully able to participate, and ultimately able to thrive.
Learn more
.**

**Benefits we offer to help you be well and thrive:**

* Competitive compensation
* Generous 401(k) program in the US and similar programs in international
* Health benefits and programs that support both your physical and mental well-being
* Flexible work environment, depending on your role
* Meaningful opportunities to keep learning and growing
* Half-day Fridays, depending on your location

Please apply directly to our job postings and do not submit your resume to any person via text message. Clorox does not conduct text-based interviews and encourages you to be cautious of anyone posing as a Clorox recruiter via unsolicited texts during these uncertain times.

To all recruitment agencies: Clorox (and its brand families) does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to Clorox employees, including any members of our leadership team. Clorox is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

data.jd_text:
Senior Program Manager
  
Meet myZoi
  
myZoi is changing lives for the better for those who deserve it the most. We are an exciting fintech start-up aiming to promote financial inclusion globally. Our vision is to provide a level playing field to the unbanked and the underbanked in accessing essential financial services in an affordable, convenient, and transparent fashion. We are looking for smart, ambitious, and purpose-driven individuals to join us in this journey.
  
Position
  

The Senior Program Manager is responsible for leading complex, cross-functional programs that deliver and enhance myZoi’s product ecosystem. Reporting into the Enterprise PMO, the role translates strategic, product, and regulatory objectives into structured programs comprising multiple interdependent projects.

The role requires strong understanding of end-to-end fintech product delivery, customer journeys, regulatory constraints, and operational impacts, combined with disciplined governance and execution.

Key Responsibilities
  
  
**Program Leadership & Delivery**

* Lead end-to-end delivery of large, multi-workstream programs spanning myZoi’s product landscape.
* Structure programs into clearly defined projects, workstreams, milestones, and outcomes.
* Ensure programs are delivered on time, within scope, budget, and quality expectations.

Product & Business Alignment
  
  
  

* Partner closely with Product Management to translate product roadmaps into executable delivery plans.
* Maintain strong understanding of myZoi’s products, customer journeys, and operating model.
* Ensure alignment between customer experience, regulatory requirements, and technical implementation.

Governance, Control & PMO Alignment
  
  
  

* Deliver programs in line with standards, governance frameworks, delivery methodologies.
* Establish and manage integrated program plans, RAID logs, and change control processes.
* Provide consolidated, high-quality reporting to PMO governance forums and senior leadership.
* Ensure governance remains lightweight, value-driven, and aligned to industry-standard frameworks rather than bespoke internal processes.

Cross-Functional Stakeholder Management
  
  
  

* Lead coordination across Technology, Product, Risk, Compliance, Operations, Finance, and external partners.
* Act as the single point of contact for program-level risks and issues.
* Facilitate steering committees, PODs, and decision forums.

Risk, Compliance & Regulatory Delivery
  
  
  

* Ensure programs address all relevant risk, compliance, and regulatory requirements.
* Work closely with Risk and Compliance teams to embed controls into design and delivery.
* Support audits, regulatory reviews, and remediation initiatives.

Financial & Resource Management
  
  
  

* Own program-level budgets, forecasts, and financial tracking in partnership with Finance.
* Manage resource planning across multiple delivery teams and vendors.
* Oversee third-party and vendor delivery where applicable.

Continuous Improvement & Capability Building
  
  
  

* Capture lessons learned and apply best practices to future programs.
* Support Project & Portfolio Managers within the PMO.
* Contribute to the ongoing maturity of processes, standards, and ways of working.

Qualifications
  
  
Experience
  
  
  

* 12-15+ years of experience in program/project management within fintech (Digital Product)/financial services.
* Proven experience delivering complex, product-led programs in regulated environments.
* Experience in leading regulatory enabled strategic programs
* Strong track record working with PMOs and enterprise governance frameworks.
* Experience with tech delivery coordination in hybrid mode with cloud native in-house built platform/outsourced delivery model.

Skills & Expertise
  
  
  

* Experience operating within Lean-Agile delivery models, including PI planning and structured prioritization frameworks.
* Ability to apply standardized industry frameworks (e.g., SAFe principles, Agile governance) without adding unnecessary process.
* Strong understanding of fintech product lifecycles and end-to-end delivery.
    
  - Excellent program structuring, dependency management, and execution skills.
    
  - Strong stakeholder management and executive communication capabilities.
    
  - Experience with Agile, Waterfall, and hybrid delivery models and tools (JIRA, Confluence).
* Experience in orchestrating Product, Operations and Technology, SLODs, commercial, Business Development and external partners.
* Ability to understand the strategic and financial implications of trade-offs and translate them into the program delivery scope and requirements.
* Ability to challenge and support Product and Tech on architecture, sequencing, and trade‑offs between scope, risk and time‑to‑market.

Education & Certifications
  
  
  

* Bachelor’s degree in Business, Engineering, Finance, or a related field.
* PMP, PgMP, SAFe, or equivalent certifications preferred.

Personal Attributes
  
  
  

* Strategic yet execution-focused.
* Comfortable operating in matrix and regulated environments.
* Strong ownership mindset and attention to detail.
* Passion for purpose-driven fintech and financial inclusion.

**At myZoi we strive to create a both a product and a team that embraces equality, inclusion, diversity and freedom. We want people who can be themselves and bring their own brand of value to the team. Come and join us!**

data.raw_snippet:

*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, sm
data.jd_text:
**Our Purpose**
*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.*
**Title And Summary**
Technical Program Management Analyst I, Launch Graduate Program 2026 - Dubai, United Arab Emirates
  
  
Technical Program Management Analyst I, Launch Graduate Program 2026 - Dubai, United Arab Emirates (UAE Nationals preferred)
  
  
**Launch Program**
Mastercard Launch is an 18-month global development program providing recent graduates with opportunities and exposure. You will not only enhance your professional growth and skills in a global setting, you’ll launch your Mastercard career.
  
  
**Technical Program Management Analyst I**
As a Technical Program Management Analyst, you will Work across teams to:
  
  

* Organize and accelerate delivery, ensuring all teams are delivering in a coordinated manner.
* Track and manage manifest definitions, release integration, timing, scope and delivery.
* Provide ongoing updates (with data) on the state of delivery.
* Proactively manage all dependencies, ensuring all outputs are production ready at launch.

**What you’ll need**

* To be available to start from 1st September 2026
* To be a Final Year Undergraduate/Masters students or to have graduated no earlier than September 2025
* Analytical and problem-solving skills
* Strong strategic and conceptual aptitude
* Ability to multi-task and prioritise multiple projects concurrently
* High-energy, can-do attitude, very strong sense of ownership

**Corporate Security Responsibility**
All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
  
  

* Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
* Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
* Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
* Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

**Description**
Job Description
  
  
As a Program Manager for Seller Fees and incentives, you will work with Product and Program Managers and cross-functional business teams (Local and WW) to implement our monetization strategy and launch & operate various seller incentives and promotions across the UAE, KSA, Egypt, and Turkiye. The PM will drive execution and controllership of all seller promotions and incentives.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  
As the central PM for MENATR fees and seller incentives , this role requires exceptional program management and data analysis skills, ability to conduct deep dive analyses, and strong stakeholder management skills. The candidate must thrive in a fast-paced environment, balancing multiple tasks while maintaining attention to detail and effective communication with diverse stakeholders. The candidate must have or will have to develop SQL proficiency and be able to build queries and dashboards for tracking performance of seller incentives.
  
  
**Basic Qualifications**

* Bachelors degree in business, engineering, statistics, computer science, mathematics or related field
* Experience in Excel (macros, index, conditional list, arrays, pivots, lookups), or experience in building financial and operational reports/data sets that inform business decision-making
* Experience in program or project management

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in stakeholder and communication management
* Experience with Excel, including use of complex formulas and pivot tables to manipulate large datasets and interpret results
* Experience in technical document writing using Visio, Word, Excel, or PowerPoint

data.jd_text:
**Job Summary**

We are seeking an experienced
**Project Manager**
to lead the delivery of
**high-end luxury residential projects**
, ensuring exceptional quality, design integrity, and timely execution. The role requires close coordination with premium consultants, contractors, and suppliers while maintaining the highest standards expected in luxury developments.

**Key Responsibilities**

* Manage the complete lifecycle of luxury residential projects from concept to handover
* Oversee design development to ensure alignment with luxury standards and brand vision
* Coordinate with architects, interior designers, consultants, and contractors specializing in high-end projects
* Control project schedules, budgets, cash flow, and risk registers
* Ensure compliance with UAE authority requirements, building codes, and HSE standards
* Coordinate with relevant
  **governmental authorities**
  to obtain all required approvals, permits, inspections, and completion certificates
* Review and manage contracts, variations, and claims with a strong focus on quality impact
* Monitor construction quality, finishes, materials, and workmanship
* Conduct regular site inspections and quality audits
* Manage snagging, commissioning, handover, and defect liability periods
* Prepare detailed progress and executive reports for senior management

**Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Architecture, or related field
* 8–12 years of experience in real estate development or construction, with a strong focus on
  **luxury residential projects**
* Proven UAE project experience with high-end residential buildings
* Fluent in English & Russian languages
* Strong understanding of premium finishes, materials, and luxury construction standards
* Experience working with renowned consultants and tier-1 contractors
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
* Strong attention to detail and quality-driven mindset

data.jd_text:
**Director of Operations Role Overview**

As the Director of Operations at Payfuture, you will own the end-to-end operational strategy and execution across markets, ensuring seamless, scalable, and compliant processes. You will identify gaps, improve workflows, and standardise operations to support fast growth, while collaborating closely with Product, Compliance, Finance, and Sales teams. Your mandate is to make Payfuture’s operations a competitive advantage.

**Key Responsibilities**

**1. Operational Oversight & Excellence**

* Own the end-to-end operations function: merchant onboarding, settlement, FX processes, and reconciliation.
* Continuously audit processes to identify gaps, bottlenecks, and risk exposures.
* Design, implement, and enforce operational policies, SLAs, and KPIs across teams and markets.
* Ensure operational readiness for new product launches and corridor expansions.

**2. Cross-Functional Coordination**

* Partner with Product to ensure operational feasibility and efficiency in new features or corridors.
* Work with Compliance/AML and Risk teams to embed regulatory requirements into daily operations.
* Collaborate with Finance/FinOps to monitor corridor profitability, transaction costs, and margin leakage.

**3. Merchant Experience & Scalability**

* Reduce merchant onboarding time and improve overall operational satisfaction.
* Standardise workflows to support rapid merchant scaling and international expansion.
* Implement automation tools with AI/data teams to streamline compliance checks, KYC/AML, and FX reconciliation.

**4. Team Leadership & Culture**

* Lead, mentor, and scale operations teams across multiple markets.
* Foster a culture of accountability, speed, and process excellence.
* Build operational dashboards and reporting frameworks to monitor KPIs, trends, and risk exposure.

**What Makes You a Good Fit**

* 8–12+ years in operations or fintech payments, with experience in cross-border payments or FX.
* Proven track record of building scalable operations and operational excellence in fast-growing companies.
* Strong understanding of regulatory, compliance, and risk requirements across multiple markets.
* Experience leading cross-functional teams and implementing process improvements at scale.
* Commercially aware: understands the impact of operational efficiency on margins, corridor growth, and merchant ROI.
* Highly organised, detail-oriented, and proactive in identifying and closing operational gaps.
* Aligned with Payfuture’s values of Belief, Positivity, Accountability & Ownership, Speed, and Execution.

data.title: Senior Manager Project Controls
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Description**
**Job Overview:**
The Senior Manager Project Controls requires an in-depth understanding of Project Controls concepts, theories, principles, and basic knowledge of other related disciplines. The Senior Manager of Project Controls must be able to apply industry standards to improv
data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**Job Overview:**
The Senior Manager Project Controls requires an in-depth understanding of Project Controls concepts, theories, principles, and basic knowledge of other related disciplines. The Senior Manager of Project Controls must be able to apply industry standards to improve effectiveness, provide guidance, and influence process improvements. The Senior Manager of Project Controls is required to identify and resolve technical, operational, and organizational problems that negatively impact the organization. Decisions made by the Senior Manager of Project Controls should be guided by internal policies, best practices, budgets, and the business plan.
  
  
**Responsibilities**
**Key Tasks and Responsibilities:**

* Oversee and manage Project Controls activities throughout the EPFCI project lifecycle
* Must have a thorough understanding of the contract requirements of the project they are assigned to
* Provide planning supervision and direction
* Directly work with the Project Manager/Director to provide visibility into the project schedule, its status, forecast completion dates, and provide alternatives in cases where mitigation is required
* Develop and implement comprehensive cost control strategies, policies, and procedures in line with industry standards and best practices
* Collaborate with project managers, engineers, and stakeholders to establish schedule and cost control baselines and ensure adherence to budgetary and contractual constraints
* Monitor project expenditures, including material costs, labor, expenses, and subcontract cost to identify cost-saving opportunities and mitigate potential overruns
* Conduct regular cost performance reviews and variance analyses to assess project financial health and provide actionable recommendations for cost optimization
* Implement efficient cost-reporting systems and tools to provide accurate and timely financial information
* Lead a team of Project Controls professionals by providing guidance, training, and performance evaluations to ensure high-quality deliverables and continuous improvement
* Collaborate with procurement and contract management teams to evaluate and negotiate vendor contracts, ensuring cost-effectiveness and compliance with project requirements
* Ensure adherence to McDermott systems and processes
* Ensure that the project develops and maintains an overall integrated Level 3 schedule
* Proactively manage the schedule; review and use late and look ahead reports to achieve the same
* Progress the schedule on an ongoing basis and analyze the impact
* Proactively manage all functional deliverables using the look-ahead process
* Liaise with the Project Functional Managers and critical vendors to ensure that the EPFCI schedule is developed at the required level of detail that will enable proper management and execution of the scope of work
* Ensure Risk & Opportunity strategies are communicated effectively and understood across the organization
* Promote the active management and harvesting of opportunities across projects
* Monitor for effectiveness and assist in the implementation of the Business Management System
* Support duties related to Risk & Opportunity function, as directed by the Project Director
* Execution Risk Management

+ Facilitate the risk management process for projects in execution supporting the mitigation of risks identified in the tender stage and continuous identification of risks along the project execution path
+ Roll out of project risk & opportunity management program to project team ensuring that roles, responsibilities, requirements, deliverables, and expectations are understood and agreed
+ Maintain a schedule of monthly engagements with the project team and management to ensure timely and accurate updating of the risk register and associated mitigation activities
+ Execute service agreement with the project manager to assure full alignment on how risk management will be applied to the project and any project-specific deliverables
+ Provide management with a strategic view of key risks impacting cost and schedule

* Support monthly CSRA (cost & schedule risk analysis)
* Own and lead the implementation, enforcement, and verification of compliance with all McDermott policies and procedures
* Ensure employees understand their responsibility and authority to effectively implement the requirements of all McDermott policies and procedures
* Ensure the timely completion of all mandatory training by themselves and their teams

**Qualifications**
**Essential Qualifications and Education:**

* Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Business Administration, or a related field
* Proven experience of at least 14+ years in cost control or project controls roles within the EPCI industry
* Strong knowledge of project cost control methodologies, including earned value management (EVM), cost forecasting, and risk analysis
* Proficient in using industry-standard cost control software and tools (e.g., Primavera P6, SAP, Excel) to analyze data, generate reports, and support decision-making processes
* Excellent analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex financial data and provide actionable insights
* Demonstrated leadership abilities, including experience in managing and developing an effective team
* Communication and presentation skills, both written and spoken
* Computer literate in Microsoft programs: Word, Access, PowerPoint, Excel, Project

**About Us**
Our ingenuity fuels daily life. Together, we’ve forged some of the most trusted partnerships across the energy value chain to make what was once just an idea a reality: laying subsea infrastructure thousands of feet below sea level, installing platforms hundreds of miles from shore, using our expertise to design and build offshore wind infrastructure, and reshaping the onshore landscape to deliver the energy products the world needs safely and sustainably.
  
  
For more than 100 years, we've been making the impossible possible. Today, we're driving the energy transition with more than 30,000 of the brightest minds across 54 countries.

data.title: Technical Project Manager, Payments Consulting
data.raw_snippet:
**Technical Project Manager, Payments**

Fime is a globally recognised international player providing deep expertise across digital payments, mobility services, and digital identity. We work with leading organisations worldwide to solve complex challenges, identify emerging opportunities, and future
data.jd_text:
**Technical Project Manager, Payments**

Fime is a globally recognised international player providing deep expertise across digital payments, mobility services, and digital identity. We work with leading organisations worldwide to solve complex challenges, identify emerging opportunities, and future-proof their strategies.

Our team of 900+ experts operates in over 20 locations around the globe, delivering solutions that support millions of transactions every day, across global markets. What sets us apart is our values: we are curious, creative, and highly collaborative, with a strong commitment to integrity - we do the right thing for our clients, our people, and our shareholders.

At Fime, the work is varied and intellectually stimulating. No two days are the same, and you will be surrounded by smart, engaged colleagues who value learning and continuous improvement.

**Move. Pay. Trust.**

**Where identity, money, and movement connect.**

**MISSION**

As a Technical Project Manager, Payments your mission is to successfully plan, execute, and deliver projects while ensuring adherence to scope, timeline, budget, and quality standards. You will lead project teams, manage resources and risks, and maintain effective communication with stakeholders (after identifying internal & external stakeholders). By applying Fime – Consult Hyperion project management framework (PM@CHyp) principles, you will oversee project execution, monitor progress, and address deviations promptly. Your goal is to achieve the project objectives, optimize resource utilization, and foster a positive team dynamic. Throughout the project lifecycle, you will proactively manage risks, ensure stakeholder engagement, and implement quality control measures. Ultimately, your mission is to drive the project successfully, promote continuous improvement, and deliver valuable outcomes that meet stakeholder expectations.

Projects scope includes delivering consultancy services and certification test platforms to the Payment, Smart Mobility and Digital Identity ecosystems stakeholders. Main customers are International and Domestic Payment Schemes, Payment Service Providers, Financial Institutions, Transport Authorities, Digital Identity Service Provider.

**ACTIVITIES**

* **Project Planning**
  : Collaborate with stakeholders to define project goals, objectives, scope, and deliverables. Develop a comprehensive project plan, including task breakdown, timeline, resource allocation, and risk management strategies.
* **Team Management**
  : Assemble and lead project teams, assign activities, provide guidance, and foster collaboration. Ensure effective communication, resolve conflicts, and motivate team members to achieve project objectives.
* **Project Execution**
  : Oversee the execution of project activities, ensuring that tasks are completed as per the defined plan. Monitor progress, track milestones, and manage any deviations or issues that arise during project implementation.
* **Scope and Change Management**
  : Manage project scope by defining and controlling project boundaries. Evaluate change requests, assess their impact, and implement changes while maintaining project objectives and minimizing scope creep.
* **Stakeholder Management**
  : Engage with project stakeholders, including clients, sponsors, and team members. Maintain regular communication, manage expectations, and address stakeholder needs and concerns throughout the project lifecycle.
* **Risk Management**
  : Identify, assess, and manage project risks. Develop risk mitigation and contingency plans to minimize the impact of potential risks on project outcomes. Monitor risks and take proactive measures to address them.
* **Quality Assurance**
  : Establish quality control processes to ensure that project deliverables meet defined standards and stakeholder expectations. Conduct regular reviews, inspections, and testing to maintain high-quality outcomes.
* **Resource Management**
  : Optimize resource allocation to ensure efficient utilization of project resources, including personnel, equipment, and materials. Monitor resource availability, adjust as needed, and manage dependencies.
* **Communication and Reporting**
  : Maintain effective communication channels with stakeholders, providing regular project updates, progress reports, and key milestone achievements. Foster transparent and timely communication to ensure project transparency.
* **Project Closure:**
  Facilitate proper project closure by conducting post-project evaluations, capturing lessons learned, and documenting project outcomes. Ensure the orderly handover of deliverables and transition of project responsibilities.

**JOB EXPERIENCE**

* At least 7 years’ experience in project management roles, with proven track record of managing complex projects or portfolios
* Proven experience within Payment Schemes, Issuing, Acquiring and Payment Service Providers technical environments and standards. Experience across Fare Collection and Ticketing systems is a welcome advantage

**KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS**

* Master’s degree or equivalent in a relevant field
* Project management certification is a plus (PMP or equivalent)
* Proficiency in project management software/tools
* Customer oriented attitude and good interpersonal and presentation skills
* Outstanding leadership and communication skills
* Strategic thinking and decision-making abilities
* Strong analytical skills, Self-motivated committed and team player
* Advanced risk management and mitigation skills
* Strong negotiation and conflict resolution skills
* Ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams
* Fluency in English

**Contract:**
Permanent

**Starting at the latest:**
ASAP

**Location**
: Dubai, UAE

data.title: Director of Company Operations
data.raw_snippet:
**Who we are:**

Stake is the MENA region’s leading real estate fintech company, combining real estate expertise and innovative financial technology to deliver products that empower everyone to own and build wealth through Real Estate. Launched in 2021 we have grown rapidly, leveraging decades of ex
data.jd_text:
**Who we are:**

Stake is the MENA region’s leading real estate fintech company, combining real estate expertise and innovative financial technology to deliver products that empower everyone to own and build wealth through Real Estate. Launched in 2021 we have grown rapidly, leveraging decades of experience in technology, financial services and global real estate, to launch the MENA region’s first mobile app for fractional real estate investing.

This is just the beginning and we’re currently searching for highly talented individuals to join and help lead our expansion into international markets and new business lines.

The Leadership Team

At the helm of our startup is a dynamic leadership team. Each member brings a unique blend of expertise and passion, from strategic masterminds to creative trailblazers.

We are looking for a Director of Company Operations who will own and scale Stake’s end-to-end operating platform, driving operational excellence across all properties and business lines. This is a highly strategic and execution-focused role.

The Director of Company Operations will act as a central integrator across the business, ensuring strong coordination between departments, translating strategy into actionable plans, and driving consistent delivery. They will also ensure that new products, verticals, and jurisdictions are operationally ready ahead of launch.

🚀
**What you’ll do:**

Reporting directly to the Co-Founders, the Director of Company Operations will be a core member of the executive leadership team, responsible for building high-performing operational teams, establishing scalable systems and governance, and embedding the processes, controls, and culture necessary for sustainable, multi-jurisdictional growth. This role combines hands-on operational oversight with strategic influence, bridging the gap between vision and day-to-day execution while driving efficiency, performance, and investor trust.

**Operational Leadership & Executive Ownership:**

* Own the full property lifecycle from acquisition to exit across all investment structures
* Oversee and integrate our Business Operations, Property Management, Transactions, and Fix N’ Flip functions in the UAE
* Define and evolve the organisational structure to support scale, multi-vertical growth, and operational efficiency
* Set execution standards, service levels, and operational performance benchmarks across all teams
* Drive transaction efficiency while maintaining strong governance, financial controls, and risk management standards
* Act as the final escalation point for material operational, transactional, or delivery risks
* Maintain high levels of investor trust through operational reliability and transparency

**Cross-Functional Strategy Execution:**

* Lead company-wide initiatives requiring coordination across Operations, Finance, Compliance, Sales, Product and Marketing
* Translate strategic priorities into structured operational roadmaps with clear ownership and accountability across departments
* Ensure operational readiness for new verticals, product launches, regulatory implementations, and geographic expansion

**Systems, Data & Scalability:**

* Institutionalise scalable systems, automation, and reporting frameworks
* Ensure data integrity and a single source of truth across transactions, portfolio performance, and investor operations
* Define clear operational KPIs with executive-level visibility
* Leverage operational data to generate actionable insights for decision-making and portfolio optimisation
* Drive adoption of new systems and processes across teams, embedding best practices and ensuring operational consistency

**Risk, Governance & Controls:**

* Embed strong internal controls across transaction flows, asset management, and capital deployment
* Maintain operational integrity across regulated and multi-jurisdiction environments
* Develop proactive risk monitoring frameworks to identify operational, transactional, and vendor risks early
* Lead incident management and business continuity planning to minimise operational disruption
* Ensure auditability and regulatory alignment across operational processes

**Leadership & Organisational Development**

* Build and lead high-performing operational teams
* Implement clear accountability frameworks and performance metrics
* Foster a culture of ownership, accountability, operational discipline and continuous improvement across teams
* Lead organisational change initiatives that embed scalability, adaptability, and a growth mindset
* Translate strategic priorities into team-level objectives to ensure clarity, accountability, and execution focus

**👀 What we’re looking for:**

* 8+ years of experience leading complex, cross-functional operations within a high-growth, regulated environment. Experience may include top-tier consulting, corporate strategy, and ideally senior operating roles in scaling startups
* Strong understanding of real estate transactions and asset lifecycle management is beneficial
* Proven experience managing complex, multi-team operational environments at scale
* Demonstrated ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and translate strategy into operational execution
* Strong financial and commercial acumen
* Systems-oriented, data-driven operator who leverages analytics to optimise operational performance
* Proven track record leading operational transformation and scaling processes

**😇 Why you should join Stake:**

* Hybrid work environment
* Work directly with senior leadership in one of the region’s fastest growing start-ups; you will get exposure to all functions at Stake and have the chance to learn from the very best in the space!
* Generous equity compensation; this aims to bring a high degree of ownership into your work. As Stake grows, you grow with it!
* Budget for learning and development; we want Stakers to constantly strive for personal and professional growth
* Best in class end of service savings scheme; through DIFC-sponsored DEWS programme
* Off-sites and regular team events

Stake has a high performance culture where the bar is always rising. We bring on brilliant, ambitious and curious people who see the value in Stake and want to grow with us.

data.jd_text:
**Overview**
As a
**Senior Project Manage**
r at Linesight, you will work with highly experienced, culturally diverse, and passionate teams nurturing a strong social dynamic and a shared sense of achievement. You will gain vast industry experience that will open doors for you, extend your skillset and expand your perspective. We will empower, support, and enable you to take real responsibility in your role so that you can take charge of your career progression with us. You will be mentored and supported by industry leaders and given the tools to grow. You will also be involved in and contribute to our business strategy as we continue to evolve. For us lasting trusted relationships are everything, both with our teams and our clients.
  
  
**The Opportunity**
**In this role you will:**

* Be a visible leader onsite, assessing progress and ensuring safe work practices
* Coordinate with the client team, consultant team and the contractor on all snagging activities, including the schedule and partner engagement
* Identify issues proactively and work with the team to resolve effectively
* Implement and monitor key project processes, partnering with the client to make improvements and revisions
* Lead and attend key meetings to monitor progress, actively collaborate on solving problems and communicate impacts to partners
* Implement project procedures and use reasonable endeavours to ensure that this procedure is always observed

**We would love to hear from you if you:**

* Have experience project managing ideally from a consultant environment in the Middle East on a data centre project
* Have a project management professional certification or are seeking one . We can help
* Have a degree or comparable experience in a project management or construction discipline
* Are detail oriented and quality focused
* Are an excellent communicator verbally and in writing
* Thrive on building strong relationships with your team and clients through a shared purpose and sense of openness and fun

All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually with video required.
  
  
**About Us**
Linesight is a highly successful global project and cost management consultancy that keeps clients coming back. And for that we have our people to thank. You see we’re not like the others. We’re different. Unique. It’s our fresh thinking and focus on what matters that has led to our evolving. We are on a journey working in some of the most exciting innovative sectors with some of the world’s most prestigious companies delivering major projects that deliver a more sustainable built environment. We have an open culture and a flat structure where you can expect to be treated with genuine care, respect, and empathy. With Linesight, you can truly discover the power of team!
  
  
Diversity, inclusion and accessibility
  
  
Linesight is committed to transparent, equal opportunity employment practices. We are building a diverse and inclusive organisation, accessible to all, based on having a safe culture which enables all our people to be their true selves. We are a people business, and we understand that the more inclusive we are, the happier our people and better our work will be. We will ensure that individuals with disability are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the application or recruitment process and are accommodated in the workplace. If you require assistance or accommodation of any kind, please mention this in your application, we would love to hear from you!

* Define program vision, roadmap, funding model, milestones, and success metrics aligned to business strategy

data.jd_text:
Key Responsibilities
  
  

* Own and govern the Genesys Cloud transformation program across the bank, covering IVR, voice, digital, and omni-channel platforms
* Define program vision, roadmap, funding model, milestones, and success metrics aligned to business strategy
* Lead and coordinate multiple workstreams including technology, operations, risk, compliance, security, and vendors
* Drive migration from Genesys on‑premise platforms to Genesys Cloud ensuring minimal customer and business disruption
* Ensure adherence to banking regulatory, audit, data privacy, and information security requirements
* Manage senior stakeholders across Retail Banking, Operations, IT, Risk, Compliance, and Executive Management
* Oversee vendor, system integrator, and Genesys partner performance including commercial governance
* Govern testing, cutover, disaster recovery, operational readiness, and post‑go‑live stabilization
* Provide executive‑level reporting, steering committee updates, and regulator‑ready documentation
* Ensure long‑term scalability, resilience, and continuous optimization of the contact center platform

**Required Skills & Experience**

* Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Information Technology, Computer Science, or related discipline
* 10+ years of experience delivering large‑scale technology programs, preferably within banking or financial services
* Proven leadership of IVR, Chat and contact center transformation programs
* Strong experience with Genesys platforms such as Genesys Engage or Genesys Cloud CX
* Deep understanding of contact center technologies, IVR, call routing and cloud architectures
* Strong expertise in program governance, financial management, risk management, and executive communication

**Preferred Qualifications**

* PMP, PRINCE2, SAFe, or Agile certification
* Experience delivering cloud migration programs in regulated industries
* Strong understanding of BCP, DR, and operational resilience frameworks in banking

Key Competencies
  
  

* Strategic leadership and enterprise‑level decision making
* Risk‑aware and compliance‑focused mindset
* Strong communication skills with C‑suite, regulators, and senior stakeholders
* Ability to manage complexity across large, multi‑vendor transformation programs
