data.title: QNB3663 - Senior Vice President - Treasury(Omanization)
data.raw_snippet:
**About QNB**
Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.
  
  
QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries acr
data.jd_text:
**About QNB**
Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.
  
  
QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries across three continents providing a comprehensive range of advanced products and services. The total number of employees is more than 28,000 serving up to 20 million customers operating through 1,000 locations, with an ATM network of 4,300 machines.
  
  
QNB has maintained its position as one of the highest rated regional banks from leading credit rating agencies including Standard & Poor’s (A), Moody’s (Aa3) and Fitch (A+). The Bank has also been the recipient of many awards from leading international specialised financial publications.
  
  
Based on the Group’s consistent strong financial performance and its expanding international presence, QNB currently ranks as the most valuable bank brand in the Middle East and Africa, according to Brand Finance Magazine.
  
  
QNB Group has an active community support program and sponsors various social, educational and sporting events.
  
  
**Job Purpose Summary**
The incumbent will lead in a proactive and prudent fashion the Bank’s treasury function by managing the Bank’s cash flows, asset – liability profile and foreign exchange exposures/risk and generate supplementary income within the set strategy, risk appetite, applicable regulatory limits and approved products.
  
  
Manages activity of the trading team by implementing the defined Group Risk Management Framework in which they operate. Develops and actively pursues cross-border trading opportunities and developing a strategy to grow the business. Has thorough knowledge of a broad range of trading techniques including trading/hedging strategies (stack and roll, cross commodity), market liquidity and volatility, options, derivatives, futures, swaps, exotics pricing and trading, and has broad understanding of commodity markets. Has extensive background in risk management strategies and is responsible for ensuring that overall value and risk exposure of the trading portfolio remains within the agreed Group strategy.
  
  
**Essential Duties & Responsibilities By Dimensions**

* Oversees the development and implementation of annual business plans and target budgets for units of responsibility with emphasis on achievement of set targets/ objectives, business growth and expansion; in accordance with the Bank’s overall budget and strategy.
* Act within the limits of the powers delegated to the incumbent and delegate activities/ authority to the respective staff as required and monitor exercise of the same.
* Approves formulated, and/or specific strategies which cover balance sheet asset, liability and contingent allocation, longer term funding arrangements asset diversification, use of market products and financial instruments with risks identified, and hedging, trading and investment strategies.
* Oversees the implementation and evaluation of the Treasury strategic and business development plans and achievement of related short and long term business goals both locally and internationally.
* Approves Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) created for the purpose of performance monitoring and quality measurement purposes for the Treasury function and monitor their achievements on periodic basis.
* Implements KPI’s and best practices for role
* Promote cost consciousness and efficiency and enhance productivity, to minimise cost, avoid waste, and optimise benefits for the bank.
* Demonstrate clear understanding of the important factors behind the bank's financial & non-financial performance.
* Approves Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in turnaround time (TAT) which will also help increase external customer satisfaction.
* Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal auditors, the Compliance function, and Group Risk as and when required.
* Meets with visiting banks representatives, and maintains credit lines and services through active relationship management.
* Establishes and oversees the management of relationships with key internal and external stakeholders and managing key client interface.
* Provide feedback to Corporate/Retail Banking business units’ managers and, as appropriate, recommend course of action.
* To assist customers in all their queries on Bank’s product and seek solution to their requests
* Maintain activities in accordance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in turn-around time.
* Build and maintain strong and effective relationship with all other related departments and units to achieve the Group’s goals/objectives.
* Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal auditors and the Compliance function as and when required.

**Education/Experience Requirements**

* University graduate preferably with a Major in Finance, Banking, Economics, or Mathematics (related field of study), Masters preferred.
* MBA, CFA, ACT or related professional certification is preferable.
* 15 years diverse Treasury experience, of which 5 years must have been in a leading role in a Treasury Division, preferably in the country or with an international bank.
* Additional commercial banking experience, especially credit and market risk management would be of value.

Note: you will be required to attach the following:
  
  

* Resume/CV

data.jd_text:
**Vice President Global Corporate Coverage - Omani National -**
**36550**
Some careers prize diversity more than others.
  
  
If you’re looking for further opportunities to develop your career, take the next step in fulfilling your potential right here at HSBC.
  
  
**Role Purpose**
Managing a portfolio of clients across multiple sectors of key CIB Banking Relationships, mainly Government Related Entities, within the Oman coverage team . To provide pro-active support in deepening client relationships and improving income generation through focused business development activities, including managing the portfolio of clients independently.
  
  
**In This Role, You Will**

* Contribute to the continued development of the CIB Banking and specifically sector strategy. The client base comprises some of the country leading in various sectors such as Oil & Gas, Chemicals, Shipping, Utilities Providers, Telco, Aviation, to name few.
* Ensure prom pt and appropriate response to customer enquiries and concerns by problem solving and facilitating the process by liaising with product and support areas
* Work with line manager/ Head of Banking in identifying strategies to maintain /enhance overall relationship return, increase revenues while optimizing the utilization of credit facilities, and ensure early identification of potential credit issues
* Provide general support to the Line Manager/Head of Banking in servicing client relationships and enhancing income generation.
* Manage and develop a portfolio of own clients by deepening client relationships and improving income generation through focused business development activities
* Work with the Business Credit Office on the preparation of structured and focused credit memorandums for clients within the sectors, covering annual reviews and new money proposals
* Understand and ensure compliance with all relevant internal and external rules, regulations and procedures that apply to the conduct of the business in which the jobholder is involved.
* Maintain HSBC internal control standards, including timely implementation of internal and external audit points together with any issues raised by external regulators.
* Develop strong sectorial/product expertise and provide high quality competitor and client level analysis to support business development and credit risk assessment
* Undertake all required activities as directed by line manager/Head of Banking, or as agreed in annual objectives, in support of achieving the business objectives CIB Banking
* Liaise with members of the client service team, ensuring a co-ordinated approach to enhancing the client relationship.
* Engage with relevant product teams including GPS, GTS, FX, etc to ensure coordinated approach towards achieving relationship development objectives.
* Develop strong bonds with the decision makers of your own client portfolio
* Work diligently with Credit Risk Management to present written recommendations and manage risk
* Proactively and effectively manage financial crime risk in team portfolio. Work closely with FCC to resolve all queries and issues that might arise from time to time.
* Co-ordinate closely with the Banking Middle Office (BMO) to ensure new account on-boarding queries are addressed quickly and the on-boarding process is completed in a timely manner.
* Live and represent the HSBC Brand and Values both internally and externally
* Uphold the highest level of integrity and act as a role model for all HSBC values and business principles.
* Take an active role in initiatives outside of own role; proactively seeking different assignments to accelerate learning.
* Establish strong relationships with internal stakeholders.
* Manage Deals and Pipeline transactions within ClientVision to ensure that the transaction data and status are accurately reflected
* Monitor income generation and effectively manage the financial reporting and analysis for the client portfolio

**To be successful in the role, you should meet the following requirements:**

* Understanding of risk management and credit mitigants
* Strong business development, financial analysis and analytical skills
* Ability to undertake in depth review of industry, competitors, clients and products and provide a high-quality analysis of Credit risk
* Ability to develop good working partnerships with credit, client management and product teams
* Ability to provide strong analytical and marketing support to Relationship Managers
* Strong IT skills (including Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc)
* Knowledge of the full spectrum of CIB products and services
* Robust understanding of Financial Crime Risk Management
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Strong team player and demonstrate a high level of attention to details, strong organizational skills and willingness to be a driven self-starter

You’ll achieve more at HSBC.
  
  
HSBC is committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment.
  
  
We believe diversity brings benefits for our customers, our business and our people. Different ideas and perspectives help us innovate, manage risk and grow the business in a sustainable way. Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.
  
  
Issued by The Hong Kong and Shanghai Banking Corporation Limited.

data.title: PMO Governance & Assurance Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-qu
data.jd_text:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-quality delivery aligned with time, cost, and quality objectives. The role also drives PMO maturity by conducting health checks, enhancing governance standards, strengthening reporting accuracy, and embedding continuous improvement practices across all projects and programs within the portfolio.

**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Portfolio Governance & Reporting**

* Lead the preparation and consolidation of weekly and monthly portfolio reports, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with actual project performance.
* Review and challenge inputs from Project Managers to ensure quality, consistency, and transparency of reporting across all governance forums.
* Ensure all reporting is delivered within agreed timelines and meets senior management and governance requirements.

**Project Assurance & Health Checks**

* Conduct periodic project health checks across all programs and projects to assess performance against scope, timeline, cost, and quality.
* Identify gaps, risks, and non-compliance areas, and ensure timely tracking and closure of corrective actions.
* Provide independent assurance on project delivery and escalate critical issues impacting portfolio performance.

**Risk, Issue & Dependency Management**

* Monitor and ensure effective management of risks, issues, and interdependencies across projects.
* Highlight and escalate gaps where risks and issues are not actively managed by project teams.
* Support mitigation planning to minimize impact on project delivery and overall portfolio performance.

**PMO Controls & Registers Management**

* Oversee and maintain PMO registers, including risk logs, change control, contracts, VAPT exceptions, and lessons learned.
* Ensure registers are consistently updated, accurate, and aligned with governance requirements.
* Implement standardized processes for tracking and reporting across all PMO control areas.

**Governance Forums & Stakeholder Coordination**

* Plan, coordinate, and facilitate governance forums including project boards and steering committees.
* Ensure accurate documentation of meeting minutes, decisions, and actions within defined timelines.
* Track and follow up on all actions to ensure timely closure and accountability.

**Financial Governance & Budget Oversight**

* Establish and monitor project budget tracking and forecasting processes aligned with approved plans.
* Ensure financial data is accurately reflected in portfolio reporting and governance discussions.

**Post-Implementation Review & Continuous Improvement**

* Review and quality assure post-implementation reviews to ensure alignment between planned and actual outcomes.
* Ensure lessons learned are captured, documented, and shared across the PMO.
* Conduct quarterly analysis of lessons learned to identify trends and drive continuous improvement

**Process Improvement & PMO Maturity**

* Identify opportunities to enhance PMO processes, tools, and governance frameworks.
* Lead initiatives to improve PMO maturity, efficiency, and effectiveness.
* Promote best practices in project management and governance across the organization.

**Stakeholder Management & Collaboration**

* Build and maintain strong working relationships with project sponsors, business units, IT, and senior stakeholders.
* Influence stakeholders to ensure adherence to governance standards and successful project delivery.
* Provide guidance and support to Project Managers on governance, reporting, and assurance requirements.

Requirements
  
  

· Omani National

· Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Project Management, or a related field

· Minimum 5–7 years of experience in project management, PMO, or governance roles

data.jd_text:

To provide top-level subject matter expertise on critical steam and gas turbines, lead and manage strategic projects, overseeing all aspects of operational, financial, customer, and employee strategy throughout the project's lifecycle. The role ensures that projects are meticulously planned, effectively managed, and financially controlled. This position requires a strategic mindset to drive success across all project dimensions and ensure alignment with broader

**Key Accountabilities :**

* Ensure adherence to company policies, with a strong focus on managing operations in line with safety regulations and company safety rules.

* Take up Lead Field Service Engineer duties to support Gas Turbine (GT), Steam Turbine (ST), and Generator job activities. This includes planning and coordinating maintenance activities, overseeing documentation processes, and leading troubleshooting efforts.

* ***Schedule and Coordinate Meetings: Arrange and manage meetings, including scheduling, preparing agendas, and taking minutes.***
* ***Maintain Project Documentation: Organize and update project documents, such as plans, reports, presentations, and meeting minutes.***
* ***Track Project Budget: Assist in tracking project expenses, monitoring budget variances, and preparing budget reports.***
* ***Manage Project Communications: Help facilitate communication between the project team, stakeholders, and other departments.***

***2. Project Planning & Execution Support:***

* ***Assist in Project Planning: Contribute to the development of project plans, including timelines, resource allocation, and risk assessments.***
* ***Support Task Assignments: Help assign tasks to team members and track their progress.***
* ***Coordinate Resources: Assist in the procurement and allocation of necessary resources, such as equipment, software, and personnel.***
* ***Monitor Project Progress: Help track project progress against the plan and identify potential roadblocks.***

***3. Issue & Risk Management Support:***

* ***Identify and Log Issues: Help identify and log project issues and risks.***
* ***Assist in Risk Mitigation: Support the implementation of risk mitigation plans.***
* ***Track and Resolve Issues: Help track and resolve project issues in a timely manner.***

***4. Quality Assurance Support:***

* ***Assist in Quality Control: Help ensure project deliverables meet quality standards.***
* ***Support Testing Activities: Assist in coordinating and conducting testing activities.***

***5. Stakeholder Management Support:***

* ***Maintain Stakeholder Relationships: Help build and maintain positive relationships with stakeholders.***
* ***Communicate Project Updates: Assist in communicating project updates and status reports to stakeholders.***

data.title: Director - Wholesale Banking
data.raw_snippet:
**JOB PURPOSE:**

To originate, manage, and grow a high‑quality corporate banking portfolio by developing deep client relationships, delivering strategic financial solutions, and coordinating with product partners across FAB. The role ensures strong risk management, portfolio quality, revenue growth
data.jd_text:
**JOB PURPOSE:**

To originate, manage, and grow a high‑quality corporate banking portfolio by developing deep client relationships, delivering strategic financial solutions, and coordinating with product partners across FAB. The role ensures strong risk management, portfolio quality, revenue growth, and adherence to CBO regulatory frameworks i.e., BM 1108, BM 1170 and all other regulatory circulars issued by CBO. The Director acts as a primary client advisor and portfolio owner, responsible for end‑to‑end execution on credit, onboarding, cross‑sell, and service delivery.

**KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES:**

* **Client Relationship & Portfolio Management:**
* Lead coverage for corporates and Business Banking clients, in country and across FAB network.
* Develop Account Plans, grow wallet share, enhance cross‑sell.
* Ensure portfolio monitoring, early‑warning identification, compliance with FAB’s credit risk appetite.
* Maintain credit quality and ensure all reviews, renewals, and covenants are completed.
* Examine & resolve credit, market and operational risk aspects of the transactions in collaboration with products and enablement units.
* Prepare Key Account Plans for clients.

* **Origination & Deal Execution:**
* Identify, structure, and originate opportunities aligned with client needs.
* Prepare CIMs, term sheets, pricing proposals.
* Lead end‑to‑end execution with Credit, Risk, Legal, Ops, and product partners.
* Manage P&L responsibility of the assigned portfolio, achieving targets within defined budget levels.

* **Risk Management & Compliance:**
* Maintain strong credit discipline and covenant monitoring.
* Ensure AML/KYC compliance, periodic reviews, and regulatory adherence.
* Support internal/external audits, CBO examination, ORM, BCP, and internal compliance.
* Manage and ensure effective implementation of the business functional policies, procedures and controls covering all areas of assigned department activity so that all relevant procedural/legislative requirements, fulfilled while delivering a quality, cost-effective service.

* **Collaboration with Product Partners/Sector Heads:**
* Work closely with GTx, Trade, Markets, Treasury, Corporate Finance, etc and all other product partners and sector heads/team.
* Drive cross‑sell revenue and support NTB onboarding.
* Promote digital adoption across FAB corporate channels.

* **Market & Sector Intelligence:**
* Conduct sector analysis and provide insights into management.
* Track economic, sovereign, GRE, and market developments.

* **Operational Excellence:**
* Ensure documentation, KFS, account plans, and pipeline accuracy.
* Adhere to governance frameworks and DOA.

* **Day‑to‑Day Activities:**
* Manage daily client engagement and transactions end‑to‑end.
* Update CRM, MIS, LH pipeline trackers regularly.
* Assist in the preparation of timely and accurate statements and reports to meet the unit’s requirements, policies, and standards.
* Learning & Development – Attend appropriate on and off-the-job training programs and ensure development and skill upgradation of team members.
* Support ED/Coverage Head with portfolio and governance reports.

**QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE:**

**Minimum Qualification:**

* Bachelor’s degree in finance, Banking, Economics, Business, or similar
* MBA/Finance preferred.

**Minimum Experience:**

* 5+ years of experience in Corporate Banking or a similar area preferably managing client relationships.

**Knowledge, Skills, and Attributes:**

* Strong understanding of corporate banking products and credit structuring.
* Familiarity with CBO frameworks including BM1108, BM1170, KYC/AML, KFS.

data.title: Human Resources Director
data.raw_snippet:
**JOB SUMMARY:**

Directs and provides leadership and operational oversight of all People Innovation and Impact’s functions at American Hospital Dubai. Ensures the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of People Innovation and Impact’s policies, programs, systems, and services that
data.jd_text:
**JOB SUMMARY:**

Directs and provides leadership and operational oversight of all People Innovation and Impact’s functions at American Hospital Dubai. Ensures the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of People Innovation and Impact’s policies, programs, systems, and services that support organisational effectiveness, regulatory compliance, and employee engagement. Oversees workforce planning, talent acquisition, compensation and benefits, employee relations, performance management, learning and development, HR operations, HR systems, and government relations. Works in close collaboration with the Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) and Senior Management Team to foster a high-performance culture, support organisational transformation, and uphold the hospital’s mission, vision, and values.

We are looking for an immediate joiner.

**RESPONSIBILITIES:**

**Human Resources Administration and Compliance**

* Ensures accurate, complete, and legally compliant employee records, including employment contracts, academic and professional qualifications, licenses, visas, health certificates, leave records, and performance documentation.
* Maintains compliance with UAE Labour Law, Joint Commission International Accreditation (JCIA) standards, and other applicable healthcare regulatory requirements.
* Oversees the development, implementation, and periodic review of People Innovation and Impact’s policies, procedures, and the Employee Handbook.
* Ensures high standards of HR data accuracy, integrity, confidentiality, and compliance with internal controls, data privacy regulations, and information security requirements.

**Strategic Workforce Planning and Budgeting**

* Leads the preparation and management of annual People Innovation and Impact’s operational and capital budgets, ensuring efficient and cost-effective use of resources.
* Drives workforce planning initiatives, including manpower forecasting, organisational design, and succession planning aligned with current and future hospital needs.
* Develops and presents workforce analytics, dashboards, and reports to support evidence-based decision-making by senior leadership.

**HR Operations, HRIS, and Workforce Analytics**

* Leads and optimises day-to-day HR operations to ensure effective HR service delivery, payroll coordination, and seamless end-to-end employee lifecycle management.
* Drives the implementation, enhancement, and continuous improvement of HRIS platforms (e.g., Oracle), translating HR strategy into scalable, user-centric digital solutions.
* Reviews and analyses HR processes, tools, and systems to identify opportunities for automation, standardisation, efficiency gains, and improved employee experience.
* Develops, monitors, and delivers workforce analytics, dashboards, and KPIs that enable data-driven talent, productivity, and operational decisions.
* Ensures HR systems data accuracy, reporting reliability, and compliance with governance, privacy, and information security standards.
* Partners closely with the Chief Human Resources Officer and HR leadership team to operationalise strategic priorities and support enterprise-wide transformation initiatives.

**Talent Acquisition and Onboarding**

* Develops and oversees local and international recruitment strategies to attract, select, and retain qualified professionals in line with organisational requirements and budgetary constraints.
* Implements competency-based recruitment and selection processes to ensure fair, consistent, and effective hiring practices.
* Oversees onboarding and orientation programs to ensure new employees are effectively integrated into the hospital’s culture, policies, and patient care standards.

**Compensation, Benefits, and Rewards Management**

* Oversees job evaluation, grading, and classification frameworks to ensure internal equity and external market competitiveness.
* Administers compensation structures, salary reviews, and employee benefits programs in accordance with organisational policies and benchmarks.
* Leads the design and implementation of performance-based incentive schemes and employee recognition programs.
* Manages staff housing assignments and related policies in a fair, equitable, and cost-effective manner.

**Performance Management and Development**

* Leads the implementation and monitoring of the hospital’s performance management system, ensuring alignment between job descriptions, departmental objectives, and organisational goals.
* Partners with department heads to support performance reviews, development planning, and performance improvement initiatives.
* Utilises performance data and workforce metrics to support continuous improvement and organisational effectiveness.
* Oversees succession planning frameworks to strengthen leadership pipelines and ensure continuity in critical roles.

**Learning and Leadership Development**

* Oversees employee orientation, learning, training, and competency development programs in line with accreditation, clinical, and professional standards.
* Supports leadership development initiatives to enhance management capability and prepare high-potential employees for future roles.
* Collaborates with Learning and Development teams to align training programs with organisational strategy and clinical excellence priorities.

**Employee Relations and Engagement**

* Fosters a positive organisational culture through transparent communication and effective engagement across all staff levels.
* Leads employee engagement, wellness, and recognition initiatives to enhance morale, retention, and job satisfaction.
* Oversees employee grievance management, ensuring issues are addressed fairly, confidentially, and in compliance with legal and organisational requirements.
* Supports staff welfare initiatives, including employee committees and engagement forums.

**Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion**

* Promotes a culture of fairness, respect, inclusion, and equal opportunity across the organisation.
* Ensures equitable practices in recruitment, promotion, compensation, and recognition.
* Drives Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion initiatives aligned with organisational values and international HR best practices.

**Government Relations and Licensing**

* Ensures timely processing of employee-related government requirements, including visas, Emirates ID, and professional licensing.
* Maintains effective working relationships with regulatory bodies such as the Dubai Health Authority (DHA) and other relevant authorities.

**QUALIFICATIONS:**

* Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources Management, Business Administration, or related field.
* Master’s Degree in Human Resources Management or a related field is preferred.
* Professional HR certifications
  *(e.g., CIPD, SHRM, or HRCI)*
  preferred

**PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE:**

* Minimum of twelve (12) years of progressive HR experience, with at least five (5) years in a senior HR leadership role within Healthcare.
* Knowledge of Human Resources Management principles, practices, and regulations, including compliance with UAE labour laws, employment regulations, and healthcare accreditation standards (e.g., JCIA).
* Knowledge of strategic workforce planning, organisational development, job evaluation systems, and change management frameworks.
* Knowledge of healthcare-specific Human Resources requirements, including employee credentialing, professional licensing, and compliance with DHA and MOH regulations.
* Knowledge of Human Resources Information Systems (HRIS), HR digital enablement, workforce analytics, and data governance principles.
* Skills in leadership, people management, and the development of high-performing HR teams.
* Skills in stakeholder engagement, influencing, and professional written and verbal communication at the senior leadership level.
* Skills in HR data analysis, workforce analytics, HRIS reporting, and the use of metrics to support evidence-based decision-making.
* Skills in policy development, process improvement, and continuous quality management within regulated environments.
* Skills in talent acquisition, retention strategies, employee engagement, and workforce planning execution.
* Skills in conflict resolution, problem-solving, and effective employee relations management.
* Ability to manage complex HR operations within a large, regulated healthcare organisation.
* Ability to align HR execution with organisational strategy and support cultural, operational, and digital transformation initiatives.
* Ability to design, implement, and monitor performance management systems and incentive frameworks.
* Ability to build strong interpersonal relationships and influence stakeholders at all organisational levels.
* Ability to administer staff housing and employee programs in a fair, equitable, and cost-effective manner.

data.title: Enterprise Architect - P&C Insurance I Dubai I Financial Services I Orient Insurance PJSC
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Requisition ID:**
177488
  
  
Established in the 1930s as a trading business, Al-Futtaim Group today is one of the most diversified and progressive, privately held regional businesses headquartered in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Structured into five operating divisions; automotive, financial
data.jd_text:
**Job Requisition ID:**
177488
  
  
Established in the 1930s as a trading business, Al-Futtaim Group today is one of the most diversified and progressive, privately held regional businesses headquartered in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Structured into five operating divisions; automotive, financial services, real estate, retail and healthcare; employing more than 35,000 employees across more than 20 countries in the Middle East, Asia and Africa, Al-Futtaim Group partners with over 200 of the world's most admired and innovative brands. Al-Futtaim Group’s entrepreneurship and relentless customer focus enables the organization to continue to grow and expand; responding to the changing needs of our customers within the societies in which we operate.
  
  
By upholding our values of respect, excellence, collaboration and integrity; Al-Futtaim Group continues to enrich the lives and aspirations of our customers each and every day
  
  
**Job Description:**
The Enterprise Architect (EA) will play a critical role in shaping and governing the enterprise-wide technology architecture for the
**Property & Casualty (P&C) Insurance business**
. This role focuses on
**Agentic AI adoption, digital solution architecture, API-first strategy, and core systems modernization**
, ensuring alignment between business strategy and technology execution.
  
  
The EA will act as a strategic partner to business leaders, product teams, and delivery organizations—driving a
**future-ready, AI-enabled, composable insurance architecture**
that accelerates time-to-market, improves operational efficiency, and supports new business models.
  
  
**What You Will Do**

* Enterprise & Domain Architecture (P&C Insurance)
* Define and maintain the target enterprise architecture for P&C insurance across underwriting, policy administration, claims, billing, reinsurance, and distribution.
* Develop domain-level reference architectures aligned to business capabilities, value streams, and operating models.
* Ensure architectural consistency across lines of business while enabling controlled flexibility and innovation.
* Translate business strategy into clear architectural roadmaps and investment-guiding principles.
* Agentic AI & Intelligent Automation
* Define the enterprise architecture for Agentic AI, including autonomous agents, orchestration layers, decision engines, and human-in-the-loop models.
* Partner with data, AI, and business teams to embed AI agents into core insurance processes (e.g., FNOL, claims handling, underwriting triage, fraud detection, agent assist).
* Establish guardrails for AI governance, ethics, security, explainability, and regulatory compliance.
* Drive reuse of AI capabilities via platforms, services, and APIs rather than point solutions.
* Digital & Experience Architecture
* Architect digital platforms supporting omnichannel experiences for customers, agents, brokers, and partners.
* Guide the evolution of portals, mobile apps, workflow tools, and low-code/no-code platforms.
* Ensure digital solutions are scalable, API-driven, event-enabled, and aligned with experience and journey design.
* API & Integration Strategy
* Own the API-first and event-driven architecture strategy across the enterprise.
* Define standards for REST, event streaming, and async integration patterns.
* Enable ecosystem integration with partners, insurtechs, intermediaries, and regulators.
* Govern API lifecycle, security, versioning, and developer enablement.
* Core Systems Modernization
* Lead architectural strategy for modernizing core P&C systems (policy, claims, billing), including:

+ Legacy decomposition and strangler patterns
+ SaaS / packaged core platforms
+ Microservices and composable architectures

* Ensure coexistence and transition strategies between legacy and modern platforms.
* Balance technical modernization with business continuity and regulatory obligations.
* Architecture Governance & Delivery Enablement
* Establish and lead architecture governance, including standards, principles, and design reviews.
* Work closely with product owners, engineers, and delivery teams to ensure architecture is executable.
* Provide architectural decision support for major initiatives, vendor selections, and investment cases.
* Act as a technology thought partner for senior leadership and transformation programs.

**Required Skills To Be Successful**
**Education**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.

**Experience**

* 12+ years of experience in enterprise or solution architecture roles.
* Strong experience in P&C insurance (carrier-side preferred).
* Proven track record in large-scale digital transformation and core modernization.
* Hands-on experience with API platforms, integration architecture, and cloud-native designs.
* Solid understanding of AI/ML architectures, with practical exposure to intelligent automation or agent-based systems.

**Technical Knowledge**

* Enterprise architecture frameworks (e.g., TOGAF, ArchiMate).
* Cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP).
* Microservices, event-driven architectures, and integration middleware.
* Core insurance platforms and ecosystem tooling.
* Data, AI, and analytics platforms (conceptual and architectural depth).

**What Equips You For The Role**
**Desired Attributes**

* Strategic thinker with strong business–technology translation skills.
* Comfortable influencing senior stakeholders without direct authority.
* Pragmatic, outcome-oriented architect—not ivory-tower.
* Strong communication and storytelling skills, especially for executives.
* Passion for AI-enabled transformation and future insurance models.

**What Success Looks Like**

* Clear, actionable enterprise and domain architectures aligned to business strategy.
* Scalable adoption of Agentic AI across core insurance processes.
* API-driven, composable architecture enabling speed and ecosystem integration.
* Controlled modernization of legacy cores with measurable business impact.
* Architecture seen as an enabler of value, not a constraint.

**About Orient Insurance PJSC**
Orient Insurance Company commenced operations in 1982 as a part of the reputed Al-Futtaim Group and has since recorded a progressively steady growth. The Company is counted among the leaders in the UAE insurance market. Orient Insurance Company has a paid-up capital of AED 500 Million which is the highest in the insurance industry in UAE. With head office in Dubai, the company serves its clientele through an extensive branch network in Jebel Ali, Abu Dhabi, Al Ain, Sharjah and Ras Al Khaimah in UAE, Muscat in Sultanate of Oman, Riyadh in Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and Bahrain.

data.title: Chief Operating Officer
data.raw_snippet:
**Overview**

DEXICONN builds infrastructure for device-backed financing.

We connect devices, software, distribution, and financial partners into a unified execution system, where device usage is controlled based on predefined credit terms linked to payment behavior.

We are building a capital-ligh
data.jd_text:
**Overview**

DEXICONN builds infrastructure for device-backed financing.

We connect devices, software, distribution, and financial partners into a unified execution system, where device usage is controlled based on predefined credit terms linked to payment behavior.

We are building a capital-light, scalable infrastructure layer across markets, partners, and portfolios. We are looking for a COO who can build and execute this system end-to-end.

**Role Objective**

Build, launch, and scale the full operational system of DEXICONN:

from device production and distribution

to activation, control, repayment, and partner integration

This is a builder role, not a maintenance role.

**Key Responsibilities**

**1. System Build (Core Priority)**

Design and implement the full execution model:

* device flow (production → distribution → activation → control)
* IMEI-based control logic
* integration with financial partners

Translate strategy into operational system and processes

Define and implement MVP (POC) and go-live model

**2. Partner Model**

Build and launch operational models with:

* fintechs / banks
* distributors
* mobile operators

Define onboarding, integration, and accountability structure

Ensure partners operate within system rules

**3. Device Control & Operations**

Implement lock/unlock logic and enforcement processes

Define rules for:

* activation
* payment-linked access
* device restrictions

Ensure full control via the platform (no manual processes)

**4. Commercial Execution**

Own operational side of:

* sales execution
* market launches
* partner performance

Ensure:

* device sell-through
* payment collection discipline
* portfolio performance

**5. KPI & Performance Management**

Define and implement a KPI system:

* sales
* collections
* defaults
* activations

Introduce daily/weekly execution tracking

Ensure accountability across all teams

**6. Scaling Across Markets**

Build a repeatable model for new country launches

Standardize processes across markets

Maintain control while scaling

**What You Must Have Done**

Built and launched a product/business from scratch

Managed cross-functional operations (product + ops + commercial)

**Worked in:**

fintech/lending/BNPL

OR device financing/asset-backed models

OR telecom/distribution ecosystems

**Proven ownership of:**

execution

results

P&L or equivalent responsibility

**Key Skills**

Strong system thinking (not task-based execution)

Ability to operate under uncertainty

Deep understanding of:

* payments and collections
* risk and repayment behavior
* partner-driven business models

Ability to connect:

* product
* operations
* commercial execution

**Nice to Have**

Experience in emerging markets (Africa, Asia)

Understanding of:

* device lifecycle
* IMEI/firmware/activation

Experience working with:

* distributors
* telecom operators

**What This Role Is NOT**

Not a corporate COO managing an existing structure

Not a strategy-only role

Not a pure operations manager

This is a build-from-zero execution role.

**Success Criteria (First 6–12 Months)**

Defined and implemented a full execution model

Launched POC and first market

Established working partner integrations

Built operational control system (device + payments)

Achieved first scalable unit economics

**Compensation Structure**

Base salary (stage-dependent)

Performance-based bonus (KPI-driven)

Equity:

* milestone-based
* earned through execution
* long-term participation

**Final Note**

We are building infrastructure, not a fintech product. The role requires focus, discipline, and the ability to build a scalable system — not just manage operations.

**To understand who we're looking for:**

We need someone who can put together the entire system—device + finance + control + partners—and launch it into production.

**About Compensation:**

Dubai-based role

50/50 salary + KPI

$15k–30k+ total monthly

Equity (performance-based, over time)

Housing + Medical + Relocation

data.title: Program Management Officer (PMO) – Binance Card
data.raw_snippet: Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an
data.jd_text:
Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world.
  
  
We are seeking a highly analytical and execution-driven PMO to support the launch, scaling, and optimization of the Binance Card program within Binance.
  
  
This role will play a critical part in vendor evaluation, financial planning, lifecycle management, and strategic growth. You will work cross-functionally with Product, Finance, Compliance, Marketing, and external partners to ensure the card program is commercially viable, operationally efficient, and positioned for long-term growth.
  
  
**Responsibilities**
**Card Vendor Assessment & Management**

* Identify, evaluate, and onboard card program vendors (issuers, processors, program managers)
* Lead RFP/RFI processes, commercial negotiations, and contract structuring
* Assess vendors across capabilities, pricing, compliance readiness, and scalability
* Define and monitor vendor KPIs and SLAs
* Manage ongoing vendor performance and optimization

**Financial Planning & P&L Ownership**

* Build and maintain detailed financial models for the Binance Card program
* Partner with Finance to develop and manage end-to-end P&L
* Forecast revenues (interchange, FX spreads, fees, partnerships)
* Analyze cost drivers (scheme fees, processing, fraud losses, rewards)
* Track actuals vs forecast and drive cost optimization initiatives
* Support pricing, incentives, and rewards strategy

**Growth Strategy & Commercial Expansion**

* Define and execute growth strategies in collaboration with internal stakeholders such as Marketing, Product, and Data teams to own and optimize the \*\*end-to-end card lifecycle

**Card Scheme Relationship Management**

* Manage strategic relationships with global card networks such as Visa and Mastercard
* Ensure compliance with scheme rules, regulations, and program requirements
* Negotiate commercial terms (incentives, rebates)
* Coordinate scheme approvals, certifications, and audits
* Collaborate on joint initiatives (marketing campaigns, product enhancements)

**Program Management & Governance**

* Drive end-to-end program execution, timelines, and milestones
* Maintain program roadmap and governance framework
* Identify risks and implement mitigation strategies
* Ensure alignment across Product, Tech, Compliance, Legal, and Operations
* Provide regular reporting and insights to senior leadership

**Strategic Analysis & Market Intelligence**

* Conduct market benchmarking for crypto and fintech card programs
* Analyze competitor products, pricing, and rewards structures
* Evaluate new business models and partnership opportunities

**Requirements**

* Proven enterprise-level business development experience with the ability to engage and negotiate with C-suite stakeholders and manage complex, multi-party partnerships.
* Strong existing network within PSPs, acquirers, payment processors, or financial institutions in the target region.
* Demonstrated ability to operate in regulated environments and navigate compliance and reputational considerations when structuring partnerships.
* Commercially mature, resilient, and execution-driven, with the ability to build momentum in high-growth, expansion-focused environments rather than relying on established infrastructure.
* Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret merchant performance data and translate insights into scalable partnership strategies.
* Fluency in English and Spanish is required to be able to coordinate with overseas partners and stakeholders. Additional languages would be an advantage.

**Why Binance**

* Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem
* Collaborate with world-class talent in a user-centric global organization with a flat structure
* Tackle unique, fast-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment
* Thrive in a results-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning
* Competitive salary and company benefits
* Work-from-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team)

Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success.
  
  
*By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our*
***Candidate Privacy Notice***
*.*
We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

data.jd_text:
Finance
  
  

* Emirates Hills

Head Of Finance / Finance Manager
  
  
Build and lead a dynamic finance function driving growth across top hospitality and lifestyle brands. If you have experience, join us, this could be the start to something exceptional!
  
  
Location: Dubai, UAE
  
  
Reports to: Financial Controller / Group CFO / Group CEO
  
  
Department: Central Services - Finance
  
  
Industry: Hospitality / Retail / Wellness / Lifestyle
  
  
Employment Type: Full-time
  
  
**Role Overview**
DEAL Holdings is building a best-in-class finance function to support a fast-scaling, multi-brand portfolio across hospitality, wellness, retail, and lifestyle sectors.
  
  
We are hiring across two levels within the finance function:
  
  
Head of Finance (strategic leadership role)
  
  
Finance Manager (operational and build-focused role)
  
  
This role sits at the centre of the business during its setup and expansion phase, with responsibility extending beyond traditional finance into systems, structure, governance, and cross-functional integration.
  
  
You will work closely with PMO, Operations, and Central Services, supporting new openings, project delivery, and long-term scale.
  
  
We are open to candidates at different levels of seniority, provided they bring strong SME experience, system implementation capability, and hands-on execution.
  
  
**This Is a Build-and-lead Finance Role, Combining**

* Financial operations and reporting
* Department setup and structure design
* Systems implementation and integration
* Project finance and PMO coordination
* Commercial and operational support
* Depending on experience, you will either:

Lead and shape the finance function at group level, or dive execution, setup, and day-to-day operations within a growing structure.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**

* Finance Operations & Control
* Oversee full accounting cycle: GL, AP/AR, payroll, treasury, reconciliations
* Lead month-end, quarter-end, and year-end close across multiple entities
* Prepare consolidated financials and management reporting
* Implement and enforce financial controls, approval matrices, and reporting standards
* Budgeting, Planning & Commercial Support
* Lead budgeting, forecasting, and reforecast cycles
* Develop financial models, feasibility studies, and scenario planning for new concepts
* Conduct variance analysis and provide actionable insights
* Support pricing, menu engineering, and cost optimisation across F&B operations
* Tax, Compliance & Governance
* Ensure compliance with UAE VAT, corporate tax, ESR, and IFRS
* Manage audits, filings, and regulatory requirements
* Establish internal controls, SOPs, and governance frameworks across all entities
* Cash Flow, Treasury & Banking
* Oversee group-wide cash flow planning and working capital management
* Lead setup and management of bank accounts across multiple entities
* Establish payment processes, signatory structures, and banking mandates
* Manage relationships with banks, lenders, and financial institutions
* Implement robust cash control and liquidity frameworks
* ERP, POS & Financial Systems
* Lead implementation and optimisation of ERP and financial systems
* Ensure full integration across POS, inventory, procurement, and accounting systems
* Develop scalable reporting tools and dashboards
* Train teams on systems and financial processes
* Procurement, Purchasing & Cost Control Oversight
* Oversee governance across procurement and purchasing functions
* Implement supplier onboarding, approval hierarchies, and purchasing controls
* Ensure integration between procurement, inventory, and finance systems
* Monitor supplier terms, pricing, and cost efficiency across all units
* Support margin optimisation and operational cost control
* Accounting Structure & Financial Framework Setup
* Design and implement the full accountancy structure across all entities
* Develop and standardise the Chart of Accounts
* Establish reporting templates, consolidation frameworks, and intercompany structures
* Ensure scalability for multi-brand and multi-entity expansion
* Administration, PRO & Corporate Structuring Oversight
* Provide financial oversight across administration and PRO functions
* Support company formation, trade licences, regulatory registrations, and renewals
* Ensure alignment between corporate structuring, licensing, and financial setup
* Track and manage costs related to government fees, licences, and compliance
* PMO Liaison & Project Finance / Controls
* Work closely with the PMO and Project Delivery teams on all active developments
* Oversee financial management of projects, including budget tracking and cost control
* Support tender processes, bid evaluations, and commercial reviews
* Establish and manage project payment processes, including certifications and approvals
* Control financial documentation related to projects, contracts, and variations
* Monitor project cash flow, capex spend, and financial risk
* Ensure alignment between project delivery timelines and financial reporting
* Business Partnering
* Partner with Operations, PMO, HR, Procurement, and Brand teams
* Provide P&L oversight and performance analysis across outlets and concepts
* Support new openings, commercial decisions, and strategic initiatives
* Evaluate investments, JV structures, and expansion opportunities
* Finance Function Setup & Scale
* Foundation Phase
* Build finance department structure, roles, and reporting lines
* Establish SOPs, workflows, and approval frameworks
* Set up financial systems, banking structures, and reporting templates
* Recruit, onboard, and train finance and admin teams
* Pre-Opening & Launch Support
* Lead financial setup for new outlets (costing, POS setup, reporting structures)
* Implement outlet-level reporting from day one
* Support stocktakes, inventory controls, and operational stabilisation
* Scale-Up & Expansion
* Prepare finance function for multi-brand, multi-entity growth
* Standardise reporting, controls, and cost structures across all units
* Support long-term strategic planning and regional expansion

**Candidate Profile**
We are intentionally open to both senior and mid-level candidates, but the following is essential:
  
  

* Strong experience in SME or high-growth environments
* Background in hospitality, F&B, retail, or multi-unit operations
* Proven ability to build systems, structures, and teams from scratch
* Hands-on experience with ERP implementation and process design
* Experience working with PMO and project-driven environments
* Strong understanding of UAE financial regulations and compliance frameworks
* Commercially minded with strong attention to detail and ownership

**Experience Guidelines**
Finance Manager: 7-10 years experience
  
  
Head of Finance: 10+ years with leadership and build experience
  
  
**Qualifications**
Degree in Finance / Accounting
  
  
ACCA / CPA / CA preferred
  
  
**Why This Role**

* Opportunity to build a finance function from the ground up
* Direct involvement in projects, openings, and expansion strategy
* Exposure to a diversified, multi-brand platform
* High level of ownership, autonomy, and impact

**Candidate Requirements**

* Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field; ACCA / CPA / CA preferred.
* 7–10 years of multi-entity finance experience, ideally within hospitality, F&B, retail, or lifestyle sectors.
* Strong experience in department setup, ERP implementation, and pre-opening finance.
* Deep understanding of UAE VAT, corporate tax, IFRS, WPS, and governance frameworks.
* Proven leadership experience managing finance teams and cross-functional stakeholders.
* High proficiency in Excel and financial modelling.
* Ability to work in fast-paced environments with strong ownership and accuracy.
* Excellent communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

**Role Overview**
DEAL Holdings is building a best-in-class finance function to support a fast-scaling, multi-brand portfolio across hospitality, wellness, retail, and lifestyle sectors.
  
  
We are hiring across two levels within the finance function:
  
  
Head of Finance (strategic leadership role)
  
  
Finance Manager (operational and build-focused role)
  
  
This role sits at the centre of the business during its setup and expansion phase, with responsibility extending beyond traditional finance into systems, structure, governance, and cross-functional integration.
  
  
You will work closely with PMO, Operations, and Central Services, supporting new openings, project delivery, and long-term scale.
  
  
We are open to candidates at different levels of seniority, provided they bring strong SME experience, system implementation capability, and hands-on execution.
  
  
This is a build-and-lead finance role, combining:
  
  

* Financial operations and reporting
* Department setup and structure design
* Systems implementation and integration
* Project finance and PMO coordination
* Commercial and operational support

Lead and shape the finance function at group level, or dive execution, setup, and day-to-day operations within a growing structure.

**Consultant / Specialist ENT Surgeon**

data.jd_text:
A prestigious
**multispecialty hospital in Dubai, United Arab Emirates**
is expanding its
**Otolaryngology (ENT) and Head & Neck Surgery services**
and is seeking a highly skilled
**Consultant / Specialist ENT Surgeon**
to join its growing surgical team. This role focuses on delivering comprehensive care across
**ear, nose, throat, head, and neck conditions**
, supported by advanced endoscopic technology, audiology services, and modern operating theatres.

The hospital provides a
**patient-centric, technology-driven environment**
, offering services ranging from routine ENT care to complex surgical interventions, while collaborating with audiologists, speech therapists, allergists, and head & neck specialists.

**Role Overview**

•
**Position:**
Consultant / Specialist ENT Surgeon (Otolaryngologist)

•
**Location:**
Dubai, United Arab Emirates

•
**Healthcare Setting:**
Multispecialty hospital with advanced ENT and surgical services

•
**Experience Required:**
Minimum
**3+ years post specialist or board certification**

•
**Practice Model:**
Outpatient clinics, diagnostic procedures, and elective/emergency surgical interventions

The successful candidate will be responsible for the
**diagnosis, medical management, and surgical treatment of ENT and head & neck disorders**
, ensuring high-quality patient care and clinical outcomes.

**Key Responsibilities – Otolaryngology (ENT)**

• Diagnose and manage
**conditions affecting the ear, nose, throat, head, and neck**

• Perform
**ENT surgical procedures**
, including endoscopic sinus surgery, tonsillectomy, adenoidectomy, and septoplasty

• Manage
**hearing disorders, vertigo, tinnitus, and balance-related conditions**

• Treat
**chronic sinusitis, nasal obstruction, allergic rhinitis, and upper airway disorders**

• Evaluate and manage
**voice disorders and laryngeal conditions**

• Diagnose and treat
**head and neck infections, tumors, and structural abnormalities**

• Perform
**nasal endoscopy, laryngoscopy, and other diagnostic procedures**

• Collaborate with
**audiologists, speech therapists, oncologists, and maxillofacial surgeons**
for comprehensive patient care

• Provide
**pre-operative evaluation and post-operative follow-up care**

• Manage
**ENT emergencies**
, including airway obstruction, epistaxis, and acute infections

• Maintain
**accurate clinical documentation and electronic medical records**
in compliance with regulatory standards

• Participate in
**clinical audits, quality improvement initiatives, and continuing professional development**

• Ensure adherence to
**international ENT guidelines and UAE healthcare regulations**

**Qualifications & Board Recognition**

Applications are welcome from physicians holding recognised specialist qualifications such as:

•
**American Board of Otolaryngology – Head & Neck Surgery**

•
**UK CCT / FRCS (ENT)**

•
**European Board Certification in Otolaryngology**

•
**Royal College Certification (Canada)**

•
**Australian Fellowship (FRACS – ENT)**

•
**Arab Board in ENT**

•
**MS / DNB / FCPS ENT**
or equivalent recognised specialist qualification

Candidates must meet eligibility requirements for
**DHA / DOH / MOH licensing**
. Licensing guidance and support will be provided where applicable.

**Compensation & Benefits**

•
**Highly competitive tax-free consultant salary**

•
**Performance-based incentives**

•
**Housing allowance or accommodation support**

•
**Comprehensive family medical insurance**

•
**Annual return flight tickets**

•
**Professional indemnity / malpractice coverage**

•
**Continuing Medical Education (CME) support**

**Licensing & Relocation Support**

Full assistance will be provided for:

•
**DHA / DOH license conversion guidance**

• Credential verification and documentation support

• Relocation assistance

• Hospital onboarding and professional integration support

data.raw_snippet:
**What We Will Offer**
If you require this document in an accessible format (e.g. large print), please contact hr.recruitment@shlegal.com
  
  
We will offer you a place where you can be yourself and where there are no limits on what you can achieve. With us, you can take ownership of your career an
data.jd_text:
**What We Will Offer**
If you require this document in an accessible format (e.g. large print), please contact hr.recruitment@shlegal.com
  
  
We will offer you a place where you can be yourself and where there are no limits on what you can achieve. With us, you can take ownership of your career and have honest conversations throughout. You would join an ambitious firm with a clear strategy for profitable growth where you can get early responsibility. Here you can expect a competitive and fair reward as well as recognition based on all round performance. This forms part of our internal promise to you, The Deal, between the firm and its employees.
  
  
**Team Structure**
The Business Acceptance team is a global team operating out of the firm's London, Singapore and Hong Kong offices. It is led by the Head of Business Acceptance based in London, supported in the same office by an AML and Sanctions Manager, three Senior Compliance Officers and 13 Compliance Officers. The teams in Hong Kong and Singapore consist of a Business Acceptance Manager, a Senior Compliance Officer, four Compliance Officers and a Compliance Assistant.
  
  
**Main Responsibilities**
The role of a Senior Compliance Officer has elements of day-to-day compliance work (BAU), as well as people management.
  
  
BAU
  
  
Primarily Involves The Day-to-day Management Of Client Onboarding And Matter Opening Workflows, With a Focus On Problem-solving And Maintaining An Efficient Service To The Business. In Practice, This Will Involve
  
  

* Acting as an initial point of escalation for queries from team members, whether related to conflicts, AML, CDD, Intapp, billing / third party payors or otherwise.
* Managing workload by allocating work fairly and ensuring it is completed promptly and accurately. Where needed, you will support colleagues in prioritising competing demands.
* Identifying and resolving any issues (technical or otherwise) which affect the general file opening and / or client onboarding workflow
* The role will also include handling a share of the team's day-to-day workload, which generally involves:
* Analysing conflict search results and resolving any regulatory and/or commercial conflicts, including via discussions with partners and fee earners.
* Advising fee-earners on information and documentation required to complete CDD.
* Implementing and maintaining information barriers.
* Conducting sanctions screenings and liaising with internal SMEs to resolve any sanctions issues that may arise with clients or matters.

People Management
  
  
**Responsibilities**
As a Senior Compliance Officer, you will also be actively involved in and mentoring and leading colleagues. Each of our Senior Compliance Officers is responsible for the day-to-day supervision of a small team of Compliance Officers (a "pod"), which includes:
  
  

* Playing a leading role in ongoing training and mentoring, especially of new joiners to the Business Acceptance team.
* Ensuring Compliance Officers apply a consistent approach to resolving conflicts, AML and other issues related to Business Acceptance processes.
* Addressing day-to-day and ongoing resourcing issues.

**The Successful Candidate Will Be Expected To**

* Act as a senior point of contact to the business on complex queries relating to conflicts, AML and other business acceptance issues.
* Assist with drafting and review of Business Acceptance policies.
* Deliver Business Acceptance training to fee earners, support staff and internally within the Business Acceptance team.
* Assist with the project management of upgrades to Intapp Open, including testing of any changes and maintaining an ongoing review of the system functionality.
* Assist with lateral hire searches.
* Provide Out of Hours assistance on time-sensitive matters from time to time, in conjunction and consultation with the Head of Business Acceptance.

In addition, there will be a requirement to be available to support the Firm’s Saudi office in line with local working patterns. Please note that this position will have a working pattern of Sunday to Thursday.
  
  
**Attributes/Skills Required**

* Proven experience in a business acceptance role is essential, preferably within a law firm environment. Prior people management experience would be useful.
* Solid, demonstrable understanding of AML and conflicts rules applicable in all jurisdictions in which the firm operates.
* Pragmatic, commercial and lateral thinking and creative problem-solving skills.
* A team-first, can-do attitude and a commitment to doing what it takes to achieve results in a timely, efficient manner.
* Strong research skills and familiarity with widely used vendors such as Orbis, Accuity and equivalents.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, and the gravitas to support taking the lead in discussions with partners, fee earners and support staff.
* Proven ability to work both independently and as part of a global team.
* Experience of using Intapp Open and Walls.
* Ability to work under time pressure and prioritise effectively to meet multiple deadlines.
* A desire to build on existing knowledge and proactively contribute to changes in team processes and procedures.

**About The Firm**
Stephenson Harwood is an international law firm, and we're committed to creating 'positive partnerships' with our clients. We represent listed and private companies, institutions and individuals, building lasting relationships to help them succeed in a complex and ever-changing world.
  
  
Our focus is on five core sectors: Energy Transition, Life Sciences and Healthcare, Private Capital and Funds, Technology, and Transportation and Trade.
  
  
With more than 1,500 people, including 220+ partners, across ten offices in Europe, the Middle East and Asia, we combine deep legal capabilities and broad commercial expertise. We have also forged close ties with high quality law firms around the world, blending local knowledge with global savvy for clients in over 100 countries.
  
  
We understand the power of diversity in delivering that high calibre advice to our clients. We want to attract diverse talent and we particularly encourage applications from underrepresented demographics.
  
  

* 220+ PARTNERS
* 10 OFFICES
* 45+ LANGUAGES SPOKEN BY OUR PEOPLE

Offices in Al Khobar, Athens, Dubai, Hong Kong, London, Paris, Riyadh, Seoul, Shanghai, Singapore and associated office in Guangzhou
  
  
**Our Brand Strengths**
**GO FAR, GO TOGETHER**
**EXPERTS IN MORE THAN LAW**
**CAN DO PERSONALITIES**
**ALWAYS READY**
